programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A serial killer wreaks havoc at the hospital, committing a murder every hour, on the hour, and targeting people who have previously survived brushes with death. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse saves a car-crash victim - but she proves to be dangerously unbalanced and repays his heroism by luring him into a fatal confrontation with her ex-boyfriend. Medical detective drama, starring Charlie Schlatter

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve finds himself targeted by masked assassins - one of whom is later found dead in bizarre circumstances. Medical detective drama, starring Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester goes undercover in a federal prison on a mission to save the lives of three judges, but when his life is threatened, the team has to break him out

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter, Cabe and Happy get trapped aboard a top-secret submarine stuck at the bottom of the ocean, and battle to make it out alive before the self-destruct function activates

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the Halloween murder of a marine with a fondness for playing practical jokes. As the evidence accumulates, they begin to suspect he was killed by the exasperated victim of one of his pranks. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of two dishonourably discharged officers are found on board a boat belonging to Gibbs, and inquiries cast suspicion on his mentor, retired agent Mike Franks. The case proves to have wider implications - and the team finds itself battling to prevent an international confrontation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team delves into Vance's troubled past in a desperate effort to trap a vengeful female assassin named Lee Wuan Kai. Although she is determined to strike back at him by targeting his family, it gradually transpires that there is another deeper motive for her crimes. Crime drama, guest starring Kelly Hu, with Mark Harmon, Rocky Carroll and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is discovered during a city-wide power cut - and because Jethro and the team are unable to use their modern electronic equipment, they have to solve the case the old-fashioned way, and it soon becomes apparent that national security could be at stake. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate a government organisation dedicated to child prodigies while researching the death of a marine whose body appeared in the middle of a field disguised as a scarecrow, and realise a 12-year-old could be the killer's next target. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester goes undercover in a federal prison on a mission to save the lives of three judges, but when his life is threatened, the team has to break him out

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter, Cabe and Happy get trapped aboard a top-secret submarine stuck at the bottom of the ocean, and battle to make it out alive before the self-destruct function activates

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A couple are taking in the view from a cliff top when a car driven by an already bleeding man crashes into theirs and teeters on the brink before plunging over the precipice. Callen and the team are brought in to investigate when the driver is identified as Lieutenant Brian Roth, and they discover that the car is registered to a Hollywood socialite Aubrey Darva. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.desc: A secret marriage and missing witness keep Perry on his toes as he deals with two cases - a hit-and-run and a murder

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A baseball player stands accused of killing his wife. However, when the jury members are taken on a tour of the crime scene, the defendant is murdered with a meat cleaver. Horatio sets about solving both deaths, but discovers the sportsman's defence team may have tampered with the evidence. Suspicion soon turns to the victim's daughter, while a notepad puts a juror in the frame

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The marine son of a colonel-turned-preacher is found dead in woodlands after changing his religious persuasion. As the case unfolds, it becomes apparent to the agents that he could have been the victim of a hate crime. Meanwhile, Gibbs' father visits for the holidays. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry helps a woman accused of stealing a car and killing a truck driver. However, his inquiries lead to another dead body, making things worse

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of a radio DJ and a naval officer who were both killed during a live broadcast, only to discover there are a number of potential suspects. Evidence leads the agents to suspect the involvement of a radical group called Military at Home, prompting Tony to investigate an upmarket gated community - where his door-to-door inquiries yield an enticing proposition or two from the female residents. American drama, starring Michael Weatherly, with Nadia Bjorlin

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The community is terrorised when a group of white supremacists kill a boy and set fire to the local church. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. Callen and the rest of the NCIS team vow to get their hands on Keshwar before he can flee the country. Meanwhile, Hetty offers her resignation to Vance following Dom's death

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby becomes personally involved in the case of a murdered naval scientist who she regards as a kindred spirit. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Max's Polish ancestry proves useful when he investigates an extortion racket in Sun Hill's frightened immigrant community. The case brings him into contact with the family of a man he shot dead during his time with the armed response unit. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Max sets out to find the teenage murder suspect who has gone on the run after being tipped off about the police sting by his stepmother. Stuart voices his concern that his colleague is letting personal involvement with the family cloud his judgement - an opinion borne out when Max confronts the armed youngster without telling anybody of his plans. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max, Grace and Jacob arrest a drug dealer as he makes a deal but his runner escapes, his face concealed by a hoodie. The investigation takes a serious turn when the youngster crashes the stolen car he is driving and runs off, leaving a passenger fighting for her life. The unidentified fugitive is eventually traced to a bail hostel, where three possible suspects are taken in for questioning, but a false confession makes the detectives' job doubly difficult

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The resident magician of a social club turns to crime to fund a gambling habit and falls prey to a blackmailer threatening to harm his girlfriend. Neil and Stevie persuade the hapless entertainer to take part in a sting operation at the club but the suspect under surveillance becomes involved in a brawl and the money vanishes into thin air

programme.title: Les Miserables
programme.desc: Adaptation of Victor Hugo's novel, which follows an epic journey through 19th-century France as the nation stands on the brink of revolution. Dominic West plays the lead role of Jean Valjean, a former convict attempting to begin his life anew while all around him a desperate underclass struggles to survive. Valjean's search for a fresh start is thwarted by prejudice against ex-convicts and the suspicion of an obsessed former guard who is convinced he will reoffend. Elsewhere, an army officer returns home from the battle of Waterloo desperate to reconnect with his son, while a seamstress has an encounter that could lead to her downfall. With David Oyelowo, Lily Collins and David Bradley

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: The rural idyll of eight-year-old David Copperfield is brought to an abrupt end when his widowed mother Clara remarries. Young David takes an instant dislike to Mr Murdstone, his new step-father, and the newcomer proves the feeling is more than mutual when he send the lad away to a boarding school run by a ruthless headmaster. Adaptation of the Dickens classic with an all-star cast, including Pauline Quirke, Maggie Smith and Ian McKellen, with a young Daniel Radcliffe starring in the title role

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: David is sent to a workhouse by his evil stepfather following his mother's death. But he finds an unlikely ally in the eternally optimistic gambler Mr Micawber. Ciaran McMenamin, Bob Hoskins, Trevor Eve and Maggie Smith star in the second part of the Dickens tale

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: Now an ambitious young man making a name for himself in London, David returns to Suffolk with his schoolfriend Steerpike in tow to catch up with Peggoty, his late mother's maid. Adaptation of the Dickens classic, starring Pauline Quirke and Ciaran McMenamin

programme.title: David Copperfield
programme.desc: Micawber threatens to expose Uriah Heep for the devious character that he is, and David returns to Canterbury once again. Adaptation of the Dickens classic with an all-star cast, including Pauline Quirke, Maggie Smith, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Bob Hoskins

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Wingless Bird
programme.desc: Feature-length melodrama set in 1913, about the romantic relationship between Agnes Conway, whose parents own a sweetshop, and Charles Farrier, son of an altogether grander family. Agnes faces an added dilemma when she finds her heart divided between Charles and his soldier brother Reginald. Starring Claire Skinner and Edward Atterton

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara's condition is not improving and she is kept in isolation at the hospital, leaving her friends able to do nothing but wait for more news. Dr Turner and Shelagh assist a man being held in a remand home whose wife is pregnant, while Lucille makes some new friends

programme.title: Zen
programme.desc: An aristocrat's death leads Zen into the shadowy world of the Cabal, a secret organisation that appears to have infiltrated many of Italy's biggest institutions - and when the detective comes under pressure to close the case as a suicide, he realises he is making some very powerful enemies. Meanwhile, Moscati struggles to cope with the mounting stresses of his job, and Zen tries to further his relationship with Tania - only to receive romantic overtures from an unexpected source. Drama, starring Rufus Sewell, Caterina Murino and Stanley Townsend

programme.title: Dial M for Middlesbrough
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas and Sian Gibson return as Terry and Gemma from Draper's Tours, this time finding themselves forced to take refuge at a sinister and dilapidated caravan park after their coach breaks down. During the unplanned stay, visitors and residents begin to be bumped off one by one under grizzly circumstances. Black comedy, co-starring Sally Lindsay, Phil Davis, Joanna Page, Jason Donovan and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Part one of two. Deed reluctantly helps Jo by examining why funding for legal aid was withdrawn in the case of a soldier who committed suicide after being damaged by army vaccines. However, he risks causing a constitutional crisis when he decides the only way to win justice for the deceased's family is to accuse the original judge of bias

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Part two of two. Deed's investigation into why a soldier's legal funding to sue a pharmaceutical company was withdrawn leads to more sinister discoveries. Forces begin to work against him with shocking consequences. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw, Jenny Seagrove and Donald Sinden

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: A mother's pregnancy stirs up feelings of jealousy and resentment in her son, who suffers from spina bifida, and a fight at the newly merged Scorpions and Giants' first match doesn't bode well for the future. Steven and Jacqui patch up their differences, while Simon and Ruth still fail to agree. Rural drama, starring Sarah Lancashire, Pam Ferris, Michael Jowett and Katherine Dow Blyton

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Beth shocks Tony by revealing she has applied for a transfer, but before they can discuss it further they spot a woman preparing to jump to her death from a bridge over the River Thames. Nate eventually rescues her from the water, only to find she is carrying a bag of heroin. Investigations into the source of the drugs focus on the boxing club where she works

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Malick clashes with Serena over a patient's care, but is unnerved when she responds by giving him full responsibility for the case. Jonny infuriates Jac by doubting her diagnostic skills, and Sacha is concerned when his daughter Rachel arrives on the ward complaining of abdominal pain - but Chrissie thinks she is taking advantage of his good nature to play truant

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry's new boss, Marilyn, tells him to make firing Nathan his first job as consultant manager. Meanwhile, Ruth makes an enemy by blaming Nadia after misdiagnosing a musician. An army corporal believes his senior officer has shot a fellow corporal from his unit and the police become involved. Jack Deam and Abdul Salis guest star alongside Ben Price, Simon MacCorkindale and Caroline Langrishe

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Beth prepares for her last day at Sun Hill, her colleagues urge her to show her feelings for Nate at her leaving party. She is pleased to find herself paired up with him on a call about a domestic disturbance in which a woman is found unconscious at the bottom of the stairs. The officers are surprised to discover their prime suspect is the boxing club owner they interviewed the previous day about drug dealing on his premises

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky and Bianca are faced with a terrible dilemma. Ian and Annie become partners in the restaurant. Ian fights against Cindy's access to the kids

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky takes Biana to the hospital to have an abortion. Robbie is trying to impress Mary. Cindy and Ian argue about access to the children

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Regina and George return to Ash Park from their wedding, and are warned about their marriage of convenience by Elizabeth and Carolyn. Meanwhile, Sarah attempts to arrange Rene's funeral and is offered emotional and practical support by Roy and Doris Collins. Elsewhere, Gino accidentally finds and reads Ann's novel, and Elizabeth is invited to a function in Sydney by Douglas Goddard

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The French police ask Jim to investigate the murder of a Jersey resident in their country. The victim is Jack Moberley, a private eye trailing an eccentric lawyer at the time of his death. John Nettles stars

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Dr Jonathan Paige's first case at the health centre involves a drunken teenager in a bloodstained shirt, and brings him into conflict with DI Gillian Cramer. Nigel Havers stars

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon gets a shock when Social Security staff realise they are authorising rent payments for her old flat when she is no longer living there and her lodger is in prison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A wave of graffiti becomes a problem in Maplin's, so the staff goes in search of the culprit. Barry injures his back while trying to clean up, leading Yvonne to call on an old dancing partner to help out, while Fred learns new things about himself. Starring Simon Cadell and Paul Shane

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Arthur Smith makes his annual visit to Grantleigh, but as he happens to be a tramp, DeVere becomes concerned about his presence. Guest starring Bill Travers (Born Free)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom and Smiler arrange an interesting blind date and Truly discovers his ex-wife's new husband is coming to town. Frank Thornton and Stephen Lewis star

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: A local journalist tries to convince the people of Lighthaven there is a big cat on the moors, but nobody will believe him - until the discovery of his mauled body

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: The detective is a broken man after being accused of crimes he hasn't committed and is forced to work outside the law in an attempt to catch vigilante killer Marwood, who has made his vendetta personal by targeting Luther and those closest to him. As Stark hurries Mary into hiding for her own protection - hoping to lure the detective into a trap - the man himself continues his private investigation, realising that the killer won't be satisfied until he has punished the man responsible for his wife's death - and when he kidnaps an innocent woman, the stage is set for a dramatic showdown in which Luther is forced to make an impossible decision. Idris Elba stars as the maverick cop, with Sienna Guillory, Elliot Cowan and David O'Hara

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the mystery of a missing scientist, after the man's wife receives a series of emails from someone claiming to know what happened to him. The physics professor was last seen in 2004, on a train to Paris for a conference, and when he disappeared he was working on a potentially lucrative cold fusion project that had attracted attention from many large organisations. However, as the detectives dig deeper, a shadowy Whitehall figure warns Sandra's boss Strickland they should drop the case

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A body found hanging in the children's playground of a recently regenerated model community proves to be that of a member of the local Community Watch scheme. Suspicion falls on the chairman of the organisation Harry Wallace, a founding father who helped clean up the estate for the residents. Reid fights to clear the local hero's name, but her colleagues are not so easily impressed when it emerges he has form for accidentally killing a man. Glasgow-set detective drama, guest starring Phil McKee, with Blythe Duff

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Tara FitzGerald joins the cast as forensic scientist Eve, delving into the death of a teenage boy on a building site. Evidence is found linking the body to a youngster dumped at a hospital one night, and as the plot thickens Boyd and the Cold Case team find themselves investigating pagan rituals, stigmata and Irish travellers. Trevor Eve stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Beth prepares for her last day at Sun Hill, her colleagues urge her to show her feelings for Nate at her leaving party. She is pleased to find herself paired up with him on a call about a domestic disturbance in which a woman is found unconscious at the bottom of the stairs. The officers are surprised to discover their prime suspect is the boxing club owner they interviewed the previous day about drug dealing on his premises

programme.desc: Jacqui and Stephen settle into their new home, while Henry and Deborah get engaged. Elsewhere, Ruth announces she wants to start a fresh away from Skelthwaite, and the arrival of a mysterious tramp reopens old wounds in the community. Rural drama, starring Sarah Lancashire

programme.desc: Part two of two. As Beth prepares for her last day at Sun Hill, her colleagues urge her to show her feelings for Nate at her leaving party. programme.desc: A transplant patient jeopardises her chances of receiving a life-changing operation, but Tara is determined to ensure she gets the care she needs - even if it means putting her own career at risk. However, her plan also puts Oliver in the firing line. Meanwhile, Serena struggles to remain composed when her daughter arrives on the ward in a heavily intoxicated state. Guest starring James Dreyfus (Gimme Gimme Gimme)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry introduces a policy aimed at reducing the number of patients who unnecessarily visit the ED, but Abs and Tess have their doubts. A woman is raped by an unbooked minicab driver who is then involved in an accident. Gaynor Faye, Marshall Lancaster and James Quinn guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A lorry driver left for dead by masked raiders who hijacked his vehicle in an isolated lay-by admits to smuggling a woman and her child into the country to help them escape a violent marriage. The search is on to locate the stolen vehicle but when it is finally traced and thoroughly searched, Mel suspects her colleague Arun has tipped off the fugitives, abusing his position as a police officer

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca grieves the loss of her baby, and Ricky doesn't handle the situation very well. Mark's virus is at undetectable levels, but his good news is marred by the arrival of Connor

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca is coming to terms with losing her baby. Frank Butcher returns, making a fuss over grandson Jacque which Roy doesn't appreciate

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George consummates his marriage to Regina after she spikes his drink with morphine, and warns her it will never happen again. Sarah returns to work at the hospital, although Jack worries it is too soon after Rene's death, and Douglas invites Elizabeth to accompany him on holiday

programme.title: Bergerac

programme.desc: French crime drama. Married cops Lise and Clovis are working by Lake Annecy when a sick man, Thierry Blanchet, crashes his boat and dies. programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Krystyn Onaivi wants to give her children the sunshine lifestyle she loved as a child by moving to Australia. However, husband Joseph is more than happy with his life in the UK. A trial week in Perth is arranged to give the couple the chance to look at the homes, jobs and lifestyle on offer 'Down Under'. Whether the experience will unite or drive a wedge between the pair remains to be seen. Presented by Nicki Chapman

The hosts will also be looking to build confidence in this format ahead of the rearranged T20 World Cup which is scheduled to be hosted by India in October, and they will hope to better their 1-1 draw in last summer's series against Pakistan

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a £6,000 Italian garden for a couple in Fareham

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Tom Heap visit Yorkshire's Flamborough Head, home to England's largest onshore seabird colony and chalk caves that provide a home to all sorts of rare creatures. Charlotte Smith investigates the environmental impact increases of offshore windfarms, and Adam Henson visits a project giving teenagers a step onto the farming ladder

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Kenilworth Castle in the West Midlands, where finds include some early Meccano toys, a Special Ops radio set used behind the lines in the Second World War and a set of nursing medals that span three global conflicts. Fiona also looks into the art of silhouette-making as an artist cuts her own portrait

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: The French detective travels to Hungary to help the British ambassador find her missing family. When a body is found, the pressure is on to save Emma's two sons. The case proves to have a devastating impact on Baptiste, leading to the final breakdown of his marriage - but a chance of redemption comes when the ambassador turns up on his doorstep with fresh evidence. Fiona Shaw co-stars in the second series of the detective drama

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem (2016)
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the media-dubbed Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public. Into this melting pot is thrust well-intentioned inspector John Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. The Scotland Yard sleuth knows he has been set up to fail, but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno. He follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London. Thriller, starring Bill Nighy, Douglas Booth and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees a married couple from Worcester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win the money they need to get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a semi-detached house in Romford, Essex, a four-bedroom terrace in Liverpool and a former bank in Nuneaton in Warwickshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit an infamous 1889 murder case, where a young American woman was charged with poisoning her husband in Liverpool

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital. There are serious concerns for a baby boy who is brought into A&E feeling very unwell

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Brackley, where he makes an illuminating stained glass discovery. Experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell assist the teams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina becomes embroiled in a feud between fair workers and a gang of Teddy boys as she searches for a runaway girl in Brinford. Back at the station, she struggles to remain professional when Jack makes a shock announcement, while Fenton clashes with the new DI when a dead body is discovered - distracting them from a wider mystery. Police drama, starring Jennie Jacques, Charlie De'Ath, Ben Turner and John Bowler

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple from Buckinghamshire who are looking for a detached property in Rutland with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch, all on their budget of £500,000. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by Geoff Hamilton, one of the country's best-loved gardeners

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to the Black Mountains in south-east Wales to help Alex and Laura finish building a tiny house on wheels

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and newcomer Steven Moore start their hunt in the Scottish Borders, buying items on their journey to sell at auction in Crooklands, near Kendal in the Lake District

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill visit Liverpool couple Tony and Nayna, who love being outdoors but find their new-build garden uninspiring and have run out of ideas to revitalise it. The pair hope that with Charlie and Lee's expertise and £4,500 budget they can secure a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, while Tony in particular dreams of a centre-piece water feature

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bernie wants more diet pills but Tiffany warns they are dangerous, especially if she is pregnant, so she insists on her sister-in-law taking a test. Honey worries that Suki is punishing herself for what happened to Jags, an angry Ben plots revenge and Lola visits Isaac in the mental health unit

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre investigates what can happen to racehorses when their careers end. The industry says that racing is now safer than ever, that the number of deaths on the track is falling and that the animals are looked after in retirement. However, many horses still suffer career ending injuries, and rather than being rehabilitated or retrained for life outside the sport, race horses that have been owned and trained by some of the biggest names in the industry, have been put down unnecessarily

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at preparations for the 2012 London Olympics, exploring the search for new talent through the story of Helen Glover, who went from PE teacher to Olympic rower. Featuring interviews with Victoria Pendleton, Chris Hoy, Mo Farah, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Tanni Grey-Thompson, Nicola Adams and Ellie Simmonds

programme.title: Helen Glover: The Mother of All Comebacks
programme.desc: Documentary following the rower's return to the sport after retirement in 2016, and her selection for the 2020 Olympic Games in Tokyo

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Miles Jupp and Sayeeda Warsi joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a terraced house in Wigston, Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe, Kent, and a piece of land in Bolton, Lancashire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1935 morphine poisoning of a care home resident Ada Baguley in Nottingham. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine. Dorothea Waddingham, the owner of the nursing home and primary care giver of Ada, was later convicted of murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Paramedics and the coastguard rescue team help a 6ft 9 Norwegian nicknamed Tiny off his tall ship, while nurse Amanda treats an old school friend

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams as they search for collectibles at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Host Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the Jupiter Artland sculpture park

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Max and Fenton investigate a spate of counterfeit notes, and wonder if it is a coincidence that pickpocket Brody suddenly seems well-off. Meanwhile, Gina is put forward for a risky assignment, and the outcome of the boxing match leaves everyone reeling

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers look back at favourite inspirational gardens, including a New York skyline-inspired space designed by colleague Arit Anderson. They also give advice on planting a window box and using climbing plants to reduce air pollution

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a family from Berkshire to find a new home in Devon where the children can enjoy the great outdoors, and visits a farm that grows edible flowers

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team meet semi-retired couple Becky and David to help them convert their drab old yurt into a colourful and inviting bijou B&B

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth look for bargains in Scotland, visiting the largest lowland loch and getting a crash course in chemistry before attending an auction at Kinbuck

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Panesars are a wave of emotions on the day of Jags's funeral, with an overwhelmed Kheerat walking out and Suki overcome with grief. A desperate Callum tells Phil he has the number of Paul's killer and needs his help, an angry Karen confronts Bernie about her surrogacy plan and Lola returns to the mental health unit - only to be told that Isaac does not want to see her

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom prepares to make a speech at Jeni's fundraiser, with Carole keen to support him on his big night - but his excitement turns to shock after he discovers his mother's secret. Josh tries to prove to Ange that he is father material, and Max pushes Hanssen to take action against Abs - but the CEO is torn over what to do

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Taking part are Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: In the early hours of 18 January 1981, in a house in south London, a birthday party ended in a fire. Thirteen young black British people died. The fire and its aftermath would ignite an uprising by the black British community. This three-part documentary from Oscar-winning film-maker Steve McQueen begins by telling the stories of the young people who were at the party and the events that led up to it

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Whinnie Williams and Tyler West host this home makeover show, helping people transform their living spaces on a budget. They begin in Salford, where seven students want to give their digs a new lease of life. Whinnie's plan is to come up with a Roaring Twenties theme for just the cost of a big night out

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Whinnie Williams and Tyler West help a trio of drag queens who live together in Cardiff and want to give their living room a glamorous new look

programme.title: Fast Food Face Off
programme.desc: Comedian Josh Jones pits Kristian Nairn (Game of Thrones' Hodor) and Gemma Bradley (Radio 1) against each other in a competition to find the best takeaway in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman helps Chick with a project, Romero makes his move, and an apologetic Madeleine reaches out to Norman.

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman learns who he can trust as he tries to protect his secrets, while Romero struggles to find safety

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter E, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the residents and shop owners of Soho Road, a busy route into Birmingham, who are entering into the Bloom Business Improvement category for the first time

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: As spring arrives, the horticulturist clears away the last of the winter debris, sows her first seeds and calls an expert in to cloud-prune her box hedging - but work is interrupted by a nesting bird. There is a surge of lush new growth come May and Carol's seedlings need pricking out, and now the frosts are over, the renewal of the garden is once again under way

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Isle of Purbeck in Dorset, looking at the changes the coastline has gone through over the centuries and encountering local wildlife past and present. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the damage that is being caused to the countryside and coast by an influx of visitors and holidaymakers and the latest news on Dwayne Fields' Snowdonia adventure

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Walking the Pembrokeshire coast, from Newport to Fishguard, weatherman Derek Brockway gets wet - from rain, an overturned kayak in the sea and from being in a hot-tub with a view. He also learns how the last invasion of Britain was foiled by a feisty woman. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones explores the link between Stonehenge and the Preseli mountains and party-planner Onkar Singh Purewal visits a Hansel and Gretel-style house, covered in shells

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The competition between Brian Cunningham's heritage and Carole Baxter's contemporary veg heats up. Carole is happily harvesting while Brian seems to have deliberately gone to seed. Meanwhile, Calum Clunie shows off his giant onions, giant tomatoes, giant cabbages and ridiculously long runner beans

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates a simple version of a classic kedgeree, as well as breakfast drop scones, a chicken, avocado and bacon salad, and easy beefburgers. She also takes inspiration from a visit to a groundbreaking primary school in south London that has built a secret garden where staff teach their students how to grow vegetables and look after a brood of chickens, and Mary joins two enthusiastic and skilful Year 5 pupils in the teaching kitchen to make something deliciously fresh for their school lunch

programme.title: My Million Pound Menu
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix presents this new show which follows people who work in the food business as they seek major investment to launch their own restaurants. They have their concepts and branding - all they need now is the financial backing. First up is Epoch, comprising Manchester chef Ruth and sommelier Emily, who have an idea to pair British wines with the best seasonal produce. Meanwhile, Scotsman Ewen Hutchinson hopes to take his award-winning street food to the next level by opening his own establishment

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives by some of the best-loved TV chefs

programme.title: West Side Story (1961)
programme.desc: Two teenagers from rival New York street gangs, the Jets and the Sharks, fall in love - but it causes already simmering tensions to explode. Leonard Bernstein and Stephen Sondheim's Oscar-winning musical based on Romeo and Juliet, adapted from the stage play, starring Natalie Wood, Richard Beymer, Russ Tamblyn, Rita Moreno and George Chakiris

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose heads for Swaledale, joining farmers who look after rare upland hay meadows and enters the cake-making contest at Muker Show using a pressure cooker he has taken on a climb of Mount Everest. Paul also goes underground to try and find rare industrial artefacts left behind by lead miners in the 19th century and meets All Creatures Great and Small star Peter Davison

programme.title: West Side Stories - The Making of a Classic
programme.desc: West Side Story is one of the best-loved musicals of all time, with its timeless story and exhilarating dance and music continuing to excite audiences around the globe. Songs such as Maria, Somewhere, Tonight and America having become some of the biggest hits in showbusiness and yet the musical had an uneasy birth and was even turned away by producers when it was first put together in the 1950s. In this documentary, Strictly Come Dancing judge Bruno Tonioli and Radio 3 presenter Suzy Klein examine the origins of West Side Story, which brought together the talents of Arthur Laurents, Leonard Bernstein, Stephen Sondheim and Jerome Robbins

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Natasha Raskin Sharp are at Culzean Castle in Ayrshire to assess items including a toy car from Germany and an art deco Carlton Ware vase. Paul Martin presents

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose heads for Swaledale, joining farmers who look after rare upland hay meadows and enters the cake-making contest at Muker Show using a pressure cooker he has taken on a climb of Mount Everest. Paul also goes underground to try and find rare industrial artefacts left behind by lead miners in the 19th century and meets All Creatures Great and Small star Peter Davison

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton returns to explore more of the nation's lochs, beginning by heading to Wester Ross to find out about the heritage of Little Loch Broom. He then travels to the off-grid community of Scoraig, where he learns about violin making and wind power, before taking the plunge to explore the hidden depths of Corrieshalloch Gorge. In Ullapool, on the shores of Loch Broom, Paul learns the Russian word for parsley, and how a huge fleet of Russian fish factory ships known as Klondykers used to visit the town

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon traces the path of the northern tropic around the globe, a journey in which he explores the environment, culture, history, politics and people of 18 countries. He begins in Baja California on Mexico's Pacific coast, crossing the country's rugged interior, before heading east to Cuba and ending the first leg in the Bahamas

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day of the Open Championship, taking place at Royal St. George's Golf Club in Sandwich, Kent. An adverse weather forecast on day four of the 2019 tournament led to the players having to begin their final round much earlier than anticipated, but it had no impact on eventual winner Shane Lowry, who shot a one-over-par 72 to claim victory by six strokes

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The second of two editions celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Tom Allen, Stephen K Amos, Jen Brister, Al Porter, Julian Clary, Suzi Ruffell and Gina Yashere

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: EZ's brazen plan faces some unexpected hurdles, while Coco finds a surprising sanctuary, and Emily is urged into an outing

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: EZ agonises over the smuggling failure, but older deeds overshadow his romance with Gaby and the rocky relationships of both Angel and Bishop

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Bishop hopes an old-school stunt can salvage the plan to break ranks with rival Mayans, even as other criminal players muscle in

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom is asked to give a keynote speech at Jeni's fundraiser, but his elation is short-lived when mum Carole comes in with a burn on her arm. Is she hiding something? The cat is out of the bag when Kylie blurts out Ange's secret to Josh, while Max desperately tries to rally the troops after the disastrous findings of the Care Quality Commission

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass examine whether a young mother accused of poisoning her family in 1850 was hanged as a result of hearsay and gossip. Sarah Chesham had gained a reputation of a serial poisoner three years earlier when she was accused of killing three local boys using poison. She was acquitted on all charges, but the shadow of these grisly events hung over her. Ordered to stand trial for the attempted murder of her husband Richard - despite little evidence against her - Sarah, with no lawyer acting in her defence, was swiftly found guilty

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks at some of the most interesting animal-related items bought by past contestants and also heads to Peterborough to discover an extraordinary collection of horseshoes

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: BBC weatherman Peter Gibbs makes an emotional return to the British Antarctic Survey's Halley Research Station, where he lived and worked in the early 1980s. During that period, the station was key to the discovery of the ozone hole, and today it is making vital findings about how people's lives are vulnerable to the sun's activities, as well as studying interplanetary travel and the threat of man-man climate change. In addition to returning to the place he once called home, Peter is on a rescue mission, since the station is under threat of being cast adrift on a huge iceberg

programme.title: Scott of the Antarctic (1948)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following explorer Robert Falcon Scott's failed expedition to be the first to reach the South Pole. The extreme conditions, poor decision-making and the pressure of the race to make it to the Pole before their Norwegian rivals proved too much for the British team, resulting in tragedy in 1912. Starring John Mills, Derek Bond, James Robertson Justice, Kenneth More and John Gregson

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to the Democratic Republic of Congo in the hope of finding the gorilla family again, and discovering the outcome of silverback Chimanuka's confrontation with a rival. He soon finds the apes, but discovers their lives are now under threat from poachers, and works with the local rangers to prevent them coming to harm

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, and Paul Martin learns about Lord Lothian's key role in getting America involved in the Second World War

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ellie Taylor, Steve Pemberton, Fern Britton and Josh Widdicombe test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble find out what happens when a silverback gorilla has his authority threatened and meet a new pancake tortoise. Jean Johansson falls for a fennec fox looking for a partner.

programme.title: Gold Town
programme.desc: The delays in building the processing plant for the gold mine continue, with unrelenting rain making the ground too dangerous for the contractors to operate their heavy machinery, while excess peat keeps being discovered. Four miles west in the village of Tyndrum, the quieter winter months provide time for staff training at the Green Welly Stop, which Fiona is keen to keep open to provide a service for those who need fuel and supplies

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as the Gamemakers take on the Animal Lovers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues asking to complete the sequence Bow, Pathy, and Tropy

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the second of the academic quiz's first-round matches, four students from University College London pit their wits against a team from St Hilda's College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Killing Escobar
programme.desc: The true story of Scottish mercenary Peter McAleese, who was hired to kill Colombian drug lord Pablo Escobar, offering a full account of that fateful mission and the dark consequences for those involved, and featuring exclusive interviews, archive footage and drama reconstruction. In 1989, former Parachute Regiment member McAleese and his friend Dave Tomkins were hired by a Colombian drug cartel to undertake a mission to kill Esobar, the world's biggest cocaine dealer, and he gathered a select team of ex-special forces operatives and began training for a lightning raid on Escobar's hacienda. After 11 weeks of intense preparation, the men launched the operation that ultimately led to disaster and would have a devastating effect on McAleese and others

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates visits Shanghai and Chengdu in search of underground Chinese culture, meeting the artists who are pushing the limits of government censorship. He discovers how the Chinese Communist Party has capitalised on the popularity of hip-hop to promote their own agenda, and investigates how Western influences have found a foothold in the country in recent years, with both positive and negative consequences

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Isle of Purbeck in Dorset, looking at the changes the coastline has gone through over the centuries and encountering local wildlife past and present. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the damage that is being caused to the countryside and coast by an influx of visitors and holidaymakers and the latest news on Dwayne Fields' Snowdonia adventure

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The Sheriffs are tasked with removing squatters from a shop in north London. James and Mark are in Birmingham hoping to takeaway money apparently owed by a kebab shop, and the Sheriffs visit a garage that allegedly damaged the wheels of a customer's pride and joy

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the brutal attack of a 96-year-old D-Day war veteran on his doorstep, with police in Taunton, quickly arresting a suspect after tracking his spending habits

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit an infamous 1889 murder case, where a young American woman was charged with poisoning her husband in Liverpool

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Brackley, where he makes an illuminating stained glass discovery. Experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell assist the teams

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don catches up with all his summer jobs in the border and the vegetable garden. Carol Klein gets inspiration for planting combinations at Cothay Manor and Gardens, and Arit Anderson learns about slugs and snails

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Brothers Tony and John Wakefield compete to become the best antiques dealer, with guidance from Paul Martin, as the buying-and-selling challenge reaches East Anglia

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Interviews with British comedy stars of the silver screen, featuring the likes of George Formby, Peter Sellers and the Monty Python team. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: The Man in the White Suit (1951)
programme.desc: A put-upon chemist's invention of an indestructible dirt-proof fibre brings him into conflict with clothing industry moguls who foresee their profits disappearing down the drain - and promptly vow to put a stop to his plans. Satirical Ealing comedy, starring Alec Guinness, Joan Greenwood, Cecil Parker and Michael Gough

programme.title: Gordon Buchanan: Elephant Family & Me
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the cameraman encounters a family of African elephants in the wilds of Kenya, and attempts to gain their trust so he can observe them up close in their natural habitat. He enlists the aid of an expert from the David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust to learn about their behaviour as he follows an elephant with a new calf, and discovers what it takes for the youngest of the world's largest land animals to survive in the face of a challenging environment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, David Harper is excited by a Polynesian war club, while Philip Serrell falls for a sewing machine dating back to 1875. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the history of the Bedingfeld family, whose members have called Oxburgh home for 500 years - and managed to keep their heads on their shoulders

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedians Ellie Taylor and Josh Widdicombe, actor and writer Steve Pemberton and presenter Fern Britton continue the daily test of their skills. Hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle find out what happens when a silverback gorilla has his authority threatened and meet a new pancake tortoise. Jean Johansson falls for a fennec fox who's looking for a partner

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin head to Japan, where they discover four extraordinary homes, built with great craftsmanship, combining high tech and a deep respect for tradition. Properties include the 'Glass House for a Diver' on the coast at Etajima, an all-glass building inspired by the chambers of an ants' nest, with rough concrete blocks chosen to surround the delicate home

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Return of the documentary going behind closed doors at the Victoria and Albert Museum in London. Theatre and Performance keeper Geoff and conservator Jo work to repair an eye-popping red lurex suit that belongs to Jim Lea, the bass player in one of biggest glam rock bands of the 1970s, Slade. Meanwhile, curator Peta and conservator Victoria are finally able to bring a 500-year-old wax modelled by Michelangelo out of cold storage, but it isn't long before they find something on one of the buttocks that takes them both by surprise

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: As the victim of a violent knife attack fights for his life in hospital, Crime Scene Co-ordinator Jo Ward is dispatched to his home in Birmingham to lead the forensic investigation into identifying his attacker. The victim is unable to tell police what happened because he has been put in a medically induced coma, and the investigation becomes more challenging when no witnesses are prepared to give a statement to the police. Jo and her team work to gather vital evidence that could help police quickly identify and arrest the attacker

programme.title: Cunk on Britain
programme.desc: Philomena Cunk continues her historical odyssey by examining the Victorian era with help from guests including naturalist and broadcaster Chris Packham, who tries to shed some light on Charles Darwin's discoveries and theories. Spoof documentary, starring Diane Morgan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the third and final T20 in the series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford, Manchester. Pakistan had never beaten England in a T20 series, having lost four of their six series against Eoin Morgan's side

programme.title: Hampton Court Palace Garden Festival
programme.desc: Joe Swift, Rachel de Thame, Adam Frost and Arit Anderson head up the coverage as the world's largest annual flower show reopens for 2021. There is a range of diverse garden designs to explore, including a lifestyle category, global impact gardens addressing environmental challenges and large show-stopping gardens and features. Also, for the first time, the presenters pair up to create a themed Hampton flower border their very own competition. With analysis and advice from Carol Klein, Toby Buckland, Nick Bailey and Mark Lane

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a complex case from Victorian Britain that featured four prime suspects, two trials, and ultimately just one man hanged for the murder of his neighbour. In 1882, 43-year old shop-keeper Harriet Candler was killed in a brutal attack, with her property ransacked

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: An elderly woman has her handbag snatched on her way to bingo - but the robber doesn't get far. Police follow a digital trail of evidence and put him behind bars

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Tom Service presents a detailed analysis of Finnish composer Kaija Saariaho's 1994 violin concerto Graal Théâtre, followed by a performance of the work by the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra conducted by Ludovic Morlot. Tom talks to members of the orchestra and soloist Peter Herresthal about the challenges of playing this demanding piece of music

programme.title: The Golden Age of Steam Railways
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the visionaries who rescued some of the narrow gauge railways that once served Britain's key industries, including its mines, quarries, factories and docks. Home movies reveal how volunteers set to work to bring the lines and steam engines back to life after they disappeared in the years following the Second World War. Narrated by Jenny Agutter

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Laurence Rees learns how and why the alliance between Britain, America and the USSR began to collapse as the Second World War entered its final stages and the fighting became more political. With preparations for the peacetime redrawing of Europe's borders, new evidence shows Churchill losing patience with the Poles and pushing them into agreeing terms with the Soviets coming from the east

programme.title: Williams: Formula 1 in the Blood (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about the Williams F1 team and its founder Frank Williams, who created one of the most enduring motor racing teams in history, winning nine Constructors' Championships. But in 1986 - at the height of this success - a near-fatal car accident left Frank fighting to survive and the team's future hanging in the balance. Featuring interviews with the likes of Jackie Stewart, Nigel Mansell, Alan Jones and Patrick Head

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Tom Service presents a detailed analysis of Finnish composer Kaija Saariaho's 1994 violin concerto Graal Théâtre, followed by a performance of the work by the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra conducted by Ludovic Morlot. Tom talks to members of the orchestra and soloist Peter Herresthal about the challenges of playing this demanding piece of music

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Laurence Rees learns how and why the alliance between Britain, America and the USSR began to collapse as the Second World War entered its final stages and the fighting became more political. With preparations for the peacetime redrawing of Europe's borders, new evidence shows Churchill losing patience with the Poles and pushing them into agreeing terms with the Soviets coming from the east

programme.title: The Golden Age of Steam Railways

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross re-creates the enchanted timberlands of America just before nightfall

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame. It is revealed how there was plenty of real-life drama and heartbreak aboard the luxury ship

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke embark on more cultural city breaks, beginning by heading to the Portuguese capital to see what it has to offer. From a spectacular monument to the maritime globetrotting of Portugal's golden age and the work of a photographer documenting the city's large African population, they discover a complex history of former glories and a darker, slave-trading past

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: As the 100th anniversary of her birth is marked by a series of nationwide exhibitions, Alan Yentob examines the life of the British sculptor and activist, discovering the effect her personal relationships and family had on her work as she struggled with a son and triplets, and charting her successful pioneering of a wholly abstract art before the start of the Second World War

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the stories of sculptors John Flaxman, Francis Chantrey and Alfred Gilbert, who bucked trends in the 18th and 19th centuries. He examines the technical breakthroughs behind their key works - such as carving in marble with a pointer machine and the lost-wax technique - which were produced at a time of increased interest in sculptural projects to celebrate the size of the British Empire

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines sculpture in the 20th century, an era of innovation, scandal and creativity. He examines the artistic revolution spearheaded by Eric Gill and Jacob Epstein, which paved the way for the work of Gilbert & George and Damien Hirst. He also explores the art form's rise in popularity, from First World War memorials to the landmark work of Antony Gormley and Rachel Whiteread

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: In a series exploring national collections at a time of enforced closure due to Covid-19, Alastair Sooke examines Tate Modern's Andy Warhol exhibition. Covering works from across the American artist's career, he explores Warhol's long-running commitment to experimental film and TV, as well as his fascination with advertising, pop music and commerce

programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame. It is revealed how there was plenty of real-life drama and heartbreak aboard the luxury ship

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: The Philadelphia District Attorney's Office charges an on-duty police officer with murder, accusing him of shooting an unarmed man in the back. The police union supports the officer and before long tensions erupt once again began Krasner's office and the force

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law

programme.title: Timeshift

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine

programme.desc: Songs and sketches about the most ruthless rulers of all time, featuring Henry VIII, Louis XIV and the Vikings

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Robert Walpole struggles to talk to England's German King, a Scottish sportsman attacks dead cows in the Highland Games, and American inventor Paul Revere demonstrates his gunpowder toothpaste. Plus, Roman Emperors channel the King of Pop in a musical argument

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Documentary following 13-year-old Nigerian hyperrealism artist Kareem Waris as he stages his first exhibition in his hometown of Lagos. The film introduces Kareem and his family, before heading to the Ayowole Academy of Art, a makeshift art studio in a poor neighborhood in Lagos. From the picture he wants to draw of his grandma in a village many miles away, to the picture he wants to draw of Olu’s brother Samuel - a child amputee - through to the fishermen of the Makoko floating village and the elephants of Oyo National Park. These poignant pictures and their settings, hope to showcase the vibrancy, colour and beauty in Africa and evoke the wonder of Wakanda in Black Panther. After the pictures are created, it's on to the exhibition space, where many of Kareems peers and heroes await, in what promises to be an emotional and life-changing experience for Kareem and his family as he finally takes the first steps towards becoming a young master.

Our celebrity mates Adam and Joe are tasked with trying to think just like Darren’s Crew and guess which way they vote on a series of hilarious Would You Rather? dilemmas. This episode features fun dilemmas, such as Would You Rather… Have ears the size of an elephant or a trunk instead of a nose? Despite his mother’s warnings, Joe zooms faster and faster, until he’s zoomed so much he finds himself lost! The Hidden Lake is a big place for a small duckling. At the same time, Francis watches his sister getting her shoes and coat on, and has a go before heading out to his baby class! programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Araiya, Oakley, Tommy, Sullivan, Samaa and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has a book inside his bag. All the parents and babies have books with different words and pictures too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate book inside a fabric bag or pillowcase to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show how they’ve been reading books and sharing stories too. The nursery rhyme This Is the Way We Read a Book features, as does a story about Baby Bear’s favourite book. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how space rockets work and how they take astronauts into space. We discover how thrust and rocket fuel work, when a rocket engine blasts off from the launch pad and into space, and learn about the different parts of the rocket and what they are for. Next, we find out about the special computers on-board that guide astronauts through space, and Maddie rides a very fast rollercoaster to show us how G-force works when a rocket launches. Finally, we find out how a rocket lands safely to bring the astronauts back to Earth.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. After a long wait, it’s finally time for Charlie and Phinehas to meet their new siblings. Both babies travel home to their families and meet their big brothers for the very first time. Once Phinehas meets the new baby, he joins in with dad to register the birth of their new baby. programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is having his first ever sleepover night with Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny in a tent in his garden, and to make it extra special, he buys a snooze cloud pillow, which has double the bounce and triple the comfort. Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny arrives, and it is soon clear they do things very differently. Love Monster has a night-time hot chocolate, while Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny drinks cold carrot juice. She brushes her teeth, and he flosses. Love Monster likes a bedtime story, while Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny prefers music. This confuses Love Monster - he thought everyone does the same things at bedtime. Then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny starts snoring and talking in her sleep and grabbing all the covers. They both end up on the floor and decide there isn't room for both of them on the snooze-cloud. Love Monster goes to his bedroom, and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny stays in the tent. But later that night, Love Monster decides to give it another try and makes her cold carrot juice, and she makes h
