In the garden, Stevie hangs out with mum and dad and listens to all the different sounds before getting ready to go out. Meanwhile, Conor heads over to grandma’s house while listening for sounds with his mum and brother. Stevie gets a quick hearing test at the hospital before heading home to make lots of noise with household objects. After making rainmakers at grandma’s house, Harrison and Conor get hold of a set of walkie talkies to play with. When the day is almost finished, it’s time for Stevie's parents to sing her a lullaby to help send her to sleep.

programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses the sparkly cake topper and remembers the day she got it. Daisy and Suki are visiting the farm, but Mrs Anderson has hurt her ankle and has to see a doctor, leaving them in charge. Magnus still thinks Ruby will love it, despite the misunderstanding. At the farm, Suki and Daisy realise why Bonnie is barking so much – Willow the goat has escaped! The hosts will also be looking to build confidence in this format ahead of the rearranged T20 World Cup which is scheduled to be hosted by India in October, and they will hope to better their 1-1 draw in last summer's series against Pakistan

programme.title: Iolo's Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Iolo searches the lower slopes of the Preseli Mountains and finds newly arrived migrant birds, including one which nests under rocks. He also hunts for the lights produced by glow worms near Martin's Haven, and watches an iconic nocturnal bird, the barn owl, hunt over rough grassland at dusk

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Tom Heap visit Yorkshire's Flamborough Head, home to England's largest onshore seabird colony and chalk caves that provide a home to all sorts of rare creatures. Charlotte Smith investigates the environmental impact increases of offshore windfarms, and Adam Henson visits a project giving teenagers a step onto the farming ladder

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Kenilworth Castle in the West Midlands, where finds include some early Meccano toys, a Special Ops radio set used behind the lines in the Second World War and a set of nursing medals that span three global conflicts. Fiona also looks into the art of silhouette-making as an artist cuts her own portrait

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: The French detective travels to Hungary to help the British ambassador find her missing family. When a body is found, the pressure is on to save Emma's two sons. The case proves to have a devastating impact on Baptiste, leading to the final breakdown of his marriage - but a chance of redemption comes when the ambassador turns up on his doorstep with fresh evidence. Fiona Shaw co-stars in the second series of the detective drama

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem (2016)
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the media-dubbed Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public. Into this melting pot is thrust well-intentioned inspector John Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. The Scotland Yard sleuth knows he has been set up to fail, but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno. He follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London. Thriller, starring Bill Nighy, Douglas Booth and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees a married couple from Worcester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win the money they need to get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit an infamous 1889 murder case, where a young American woman was charged with poisoning her husband in Liverpool

Police drama, starring Jennie Jacques, Charlie De'Ath, Ben Turner and John Bowler

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by Geoff Hamilton, one of the country's best-loved gardeners

programme.title: Iolo's Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores a wetland oasis for dragonflies at Llangloffan Fen. He finds cave nesting birds and gets closer than ever to a hunting kestrel near Ceibwr Bay. Iolo also sets up camp in a hide to try to spot the often elusive kingfishers and otters that make their homes along the Eastern Cleddau river

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bernie wants more diet pills but Tiffany warns they are dangerous, especially if she is pregnant, so she insists on her sister-in-law taking a test. Honey worries that Suki is punishing herself for what happened to Jags, an angry Ben plots revenge and Lola visits Isaac in the mental health unit

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre investigates what can happen to racehorses when their careers end. The industry says that racing is now safer than ever, that the number of deaths on the track is falling and that the animals are looked after in retirement. However, many horses still suffer career ending injuries, and rather than being rehabilitated or retrained for life outside the sport, race horses that have been owned and trained by some of the biggest names in the industry, have been put down unnecessarily

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at preparations for the 2012 London Olympics, exploring the search for new talent through the story of Helen Glover, who went from PE teacher to Olympic rower. Featuring interviews with Victoria Pendleton, Chris Hoy, Mo Farah, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Tanni Grey-Thompson, Nicola Adams and Ellie Simmonds

programme.title: Helen Glover: The Mother of All Comebacks
programme.desc: Documentary following the rower's return to the sport after retirement in 2016, and her selection for the 2020 Olympic Games in Tokyo

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Panesars are a wave of emotions on the day of Jags's funeral, with an overwhelmed Kheerat walking out and Suki overcome with grief. A desperate Callum tells Phil he has the number of Paul's killer and needs his help, an angry Karen confronts Bernie about her surrogacy plan and Lola returns to the mental health unit - only to be told that Isaac does not want to see her

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom prepares to make a speech at Jeni's fundraiser, with Carole keen to support him on his big night - but his excitement turns to shock after he discovers his mother's secret. Josh tries to prove to Ange that he is father material, and Max pushes Hanssen to take action against Abs - but the CEO is torn over what to do

programme.title: Jade Jones: Fighting for Gold
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of the double Olympic taekwondo gold medallist as she attempts to make history by becoming the first British woman to win gold medals in three successive games

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: In the early hours of 18 January 1981, in a house in south London, a birthday party ended in a fire. Thirteen young black British people died. The fire and its aftermath would ignite an uprising by the black British community. This three-part documentary from Oscar-winning film-maker Steve McQueen begins by telling the stories of the young people who were at the party and the events that led up to it

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Whinnie Williams and Tyler West host this home makeover show, helping people transform their living spaces on a budget. They begin in Salford, where seven students want to give their digs a new lease of life. Whinnie's plan is to come up with a Roaring Twenties theme for just the cost of a big night out

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Whinnie Williams and Tyler West help a trio of drag queens who live together in Cardiff and want to give their living room a glamorous new look

programme.title: Fast Food Face Off
programme.desc: Comedian Josh Jones pits Kristian Nairn (Game of Thrones' Hodor) and Gemma Bradley (Radio 1) against each other in a competition to find the best takeaway in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman helps Chick with a project, Romero makes his move, and an apologetic Madeleine reaches out to Norman.

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman learns who he can trust as he tries to protect his secrets, while Romero struggles to find safety

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Devizes, Wiltshire, designing a wildlife-friendly garden that must also be ideal for everyday family life - on a budget of £1,500, with plans including upcycling old pallets

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Nina Wadia present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Krystyn Onaivi wants to give her children the sunshine lifestyle she loved as a child by moving to Australia. However, husband Joseph is more than happy with his life in the UK. A trial week in Perth is arranged to give the couple the chance to look at the homes, jobs and lifestyle on offer 'Down Under'. Whether the experience will unite or drive a wedge between the pair remains to be seen. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents as two more teams search for antiques in Anglesey, assisted by experts Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits Arundel in West Sussex to explore the Catholic Cathedral and castle gardens, and Radzi Chinyanganya meets chart-topping nuns the Poor Clares

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Isa Guha presents coverage of the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Emerald Headingley Stadium in Leeds. The hosts will also be looking to build confidence in this format ahead of the rearranged T20 World Cup which is scheduled to be hosted by India in October, and they will hope to better their 1-1 draw in last summer's series against Pakistan

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a £6,000 Italian garden for a couple in Fareham

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Tom Heap visit Yorkshire's Flamborough Head, home to England's largest onshore seabird colony and chalk caves that provide a home to all sorts of rare creatures. Charlotte Smith investigates the environmental impact increases of offshore windfarms, and Adam Henson visits a project giving teenagers a step onto the farming ladder

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Kenilworth Castle in the West Midlands, where finds include some early Meccano toys, a Special Ops radio set used behind the lines in the Second World War and a set of nursing medals that span three global conflicts. Fiona also looks into the art of silhouette-making as an artist cuts her own portrait

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: The French detective travels to Hungary to help the British ambassador find her missing family. When a body is found, the pressure is on to save Emma's two sons. The case proves to have a devastating impact on Baptiste, leading to the final breakdown of his marriage - but a chance of redemption comes when the ambassador turns up on his doorstep with fresh evidence. Fiona Shaw co-stars in the second series of the detective drama

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem (2016)
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the media-dubbed Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public. Into this melting pot is thrust well-intentioned inspector John Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. The Scotland Yard sleuth knows he has been set up to fail, but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno. He follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London. Thriller, starring Bill Nighy, Douglas Booth and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees a married couple from Worcester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win the money they need to get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a semi-detached house in Romford, Essex, a four-bedroom terrace in Liverpool and a former bank in Nuneaton in Warwickshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit an infamous 1889 murder case, where a young American woman was charged with poisoning her husband in Liverpool

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital. There are serious concerns for a baby boy who is brought into A&E feeling very unwell

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Brackley, where he makes an illuminating stained glass discovery. Experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell assist the teams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina becomes embroiled in a feud between fair workers and a gang of Teddy boys as she searches for a runaway girl in Brinford. Back at the station, she struggles to remain professional when Jack makes a shock announcement, while Fenton clashes with the new DI when a dead body is discovered - distracting them from a wider mystery. Police drama, starring Jennie Jacques, Charlie De'Ath, Ben Turner and John Bowler

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple from Buckinghamshire who are looking for a detached property in Rutland with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch, all on their budget of £500,000. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by Geoff Hamilton, one of the country's best-loved gardeners

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to the Black Mountains in south-east Wales to help Alex and Laura finish building a tiny house on wheels

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and newcomer Steven Moore start their hunt in the Scottish Borders, buying items on their journey to sell at auction in Crooklands, near Kendal in the Lake District

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill visit Liverpool couple Tony and Nayna, who love being outdoors but find their new-build garden uninspiring and have run out of ideas to revitalise it. The pair hope that with Charlie and Lee's expertise and £4,500 budget they can secure a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, while Tony in particular dreams of a centre-piece water feature

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bernie wants more diet pills but Tiffany warns they are dangerous, especially if she is pregnant, so she insists on her sister-in-law taking a test. Honey worries that Suki is punishing herself for what happened to Jags, an angry Ben plots revenge and Lola visits Isaac in the mental health unit

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre investigates what can happen to racehorses when their careers end. The industry says that racing is now safer than ever, that the number of deaths on the track is falling and that the animals are looked after in retirement. However, many horses still suffer career ending injuries, and rather than being rehabilitated or retrained for life outside the sport, race horses that have been owned and trained by some of the biggest names in the industry, have been put down unnecessarily

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at preparations for the 2012 London Olympics, exploring the search for new talent through the story of Helen Glover, who went from PE teacher to Olympic rower. Featuring interviews with Victoria Pendleton, Chris Hoy, Mo Farah, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Tanni Grey-Thompson, Nicola Adams and Ellie Simmonds

programme.title: Helen Glover: The Mother of All Comebacks
programme.desc: Documentary following the rower's return to the sport after retirement in 2016, and her selection for the 2020 Olympic Games in Tokyo

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Miles Jupp and Sayeeda Warsi joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1935 morphine poisoning of a care home resident Ada Baguley in Nottingham. programme.desc: Barra Best reveals how endangered animals across Europe could be making a return with the help of conservationists. He discovers how red kite chicks have successfully been reintroduced to the Republic of Ireland, while on the slopes of Slieve Gullion, a native creature has helped a once endangered animal fight back against an invasive species. Further afield, Barra discovers how Arctic foxes are thriving in Norway and witnesses efforts to help a loggerhead turtle in Barcelona

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Whinnie Williams and Tyler West host this home makeover show, helping people transform their living spaces on a budget. They begin in Salford, where seven students want to give their digs a new lease of life. Whinnie's plan is to come up with a Roaring Twenties theme for just the cost of a big night out

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre investigates what can happen to racehorses when their careers end. The industry says that racing is now safer than ever, that the number of deaths on the track is falling and that the animals are looked after in retirement. However, many horses still suffer career ending injuries, and rather than being rehabilitated or retrained for life outside the sport, race horses that have been owned and trained by some of the biggest names in the industry, have been put down unnecessarily

programme.desc: Ellen NicDhòmhnaill introduces her personal highlights from the craziest news show on BBC ALBA

programme.title: Ceathrar air a' Chuiltheann (Four on the Cuillin)
programme.desc: Teenagers Rosie, Zach, Mairead and Siobhan continue their challenge to tackle the hardest mountain climb in the UK - the Cuillin Ridge on the Isle of Skye

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on boys' games and girls' sports

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Slighe Chladach Fiobha
programme.desc: Iagan MacNeil explores the history, geology, scenery - and Gaelic - from the Kincardine Bridge to Newburgh

programme.title: Tarraing nan Tonn (Chasing Waves)
programme.desc: John Murdo MacAulay from Lewis enjoys a fun-filled and revealing journey into the world of surfing on his home island. Cameras capture him through the seasonal changes - from the depths of a bitter Hebridean winter, to the stunning, long summer nights

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Strokes are the third commonest cause of death in Scotland, and can be life-changing for survivors and their families. This documentary examines their cause and impact

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Linda MacLeod reads the poem “Mar Chuimhneachan air Mo Mhac Ghràdhach” which was written by her great aunt, Morag MacDonald, after losing her son

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Slighe Anndra
programme.desc: John Carmichael visits Lisbon as he traces the history of the Church of Scotland across Europe

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave wants to learn to play the drums at school, but is given the recorder. With a talent show around the corner, Dave gets practising.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave and Fuzzypeg the dog get dirty playing at the local dump, so dirty in fact that Fuzzypeg picks up an extremely grumpy flea.

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: LEGO Hidden Side
programme.desc: Parker reveals her fear of flying mid-air in Douglas' new bi-plane. Things get scarier when they meet a weather controlling ghost.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Poor Mike is bedridden. He's been struck down by a nasty cold so Iris and the turtles are forced take care of his every need, including trying to repel the raccoons from the house. Fighting raccoons isn't their specialty and Iris and the turtles quickly end up locked in the garage. Mike is forced to intervene but he's so sick and weak that he's a pushover. How can he overcome the raccoons this time? Mike's problem could be the very solution he needs to defeat the tenacious raccoons.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced to spend the day outside in his doghouse due to Grandma Mikkelsen's fur allergy

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis' dreams are haunted by a familiar foe. (S2 Ep4a)

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: AJ Odudu, Denise Van Outen and Jay Blades.join Martin and Roman Kemp for more chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: In a special edition of the show, the team transform a narrow backyard in London for charity worker Maxine, and a tiny garden for a family of five in Kent. Throughout the programme, the team demonstrate that, with the right design, plants and a succession of tips, tricks, clever ideas, small outdoor spaces can be made to look and feel much bigger

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: A chance to look back at some of the best performances from the series so far, with the opportunity to sing along to the hits

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show in which three members of the public play a giant arcade machine where everything can change on the roll of a coin, with Martine McCutcheon, Jason Manford and AJ Adudu helping the contestants

programme.title: The Void
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo and Fleur East host the game show in which contestants take on a wide range of demanding mental and physical challenges to win a jackpot of £25,000 - all while navigating the terrifying prospect of falling into a huge tank holding 520,000 litres of water called the Void

programme.title: Happy Feet (2006)
programme.desc: A young penguin disappoints his fellow seabirds with his inability to sing - but instead he displays a remarkable talent for tap dancing. However, his elders refuse to accept this outlandish behaviour and send the youngster into exile, marking the start of a wonderful journey. Animated adventure, with the voices of Elijah Wood, Robin Williams, Nicole Kidman and Hugh Jackman

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as Fern Britton, Iain Stirling and Mark-Francis Vandelli answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a Coronation Street edition of the quiz, as celebrity contestants Shelley King, Daniel Brocklebank, Kym Marsh and Jack P Shepherd pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)
programme.desc: The young wizard and his friends return for their second term at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry to discover a strange serpentine power is petrifying the other students. Meanwhile, Harry is haunted by a sinister voice in his head urging him to uncover the dark force that is stalking the corridors of the school. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson and Kenneth Branagh

programme.title: Professor T
programme.desc: University of Cambridge criminology professor Jasper Tempest is obsessed with cleanliness, structure and order, and is persuaded by a former student - now a police detective - to help her investigate a serial rapist. Meanwhile, when he discovers his mother Adelaide is trying to sell his long-abandoned family home, Jasper makes a momentous decision. Crime drama, starring Ben Miller, Frances de la Tour

programme.title: The Two Ronnies: Ronnie Corbett's Lost Tapes
programme.desc: A look back at the life and work of one of Britain's favourite comedians, Ronnie Corbett. The programme tells the story of Corbett's childhood in Edinburgh, his early career in London, and how he teamed up with Ronnie Barker to make 12 series of The Two Ronnies, and features behind-the-scenes stories and interviews with his family and friends

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of two people trying to unravel family mysteries, with both looking for a birth parent they have never met. Fifty-seven-year-old quarry worker John Hacking was told that his birth mother left him as a baby in a pram outside a block of flats in Buxton and has been searching for her since he was 17 with no luck, while Debra Spark has never met her Italian father Ernesto, who got together with Debra's mother Olive in Milan in the 1960s

programme.title: Craig and Bruno's Great British Road Trips
programme.desc: Craig Revel Horwood and Bruno Tonioli embark on a road trip around Britain, taking in some of the country's most spectacular scenery. In the first episode, the duo explore the Cornwall and some of the most most scenic stretches of coastline in the UK. Setting out from Padstow, they travel past the dramatic Atlantic cliffs and coves, before making their way around Britain's most south-westerly point to reach their final destination in Penzance

programme.title: Goodwood Festival of Speed
programme.desc: Highlights from the Goodwood Estate, celebrating motorsport's all-rounders, with stories of racing legends, as well as all the action, history and emotion from the festival

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 21st and final stage of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 108.4km course from Chatou to the iconic finish on the Champs-Elysees in Paris. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Thruxton, featuring round two of the Mini Challenge and the Ginetta GT5 Challenge

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Wolverhampton, where Henry Nicholls picks up a couple of paintings with very different price tags, while Tony Geering takes on a troublesome twosome. Simon Schneider turns horse dealer and Karen Dalmeny ends up with a very fancy clock

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends and family answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four more contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Lyndsey, Trudy, Peter and Diane must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Meena meddles. Laurel helps Nicola for her date night, and Tracy is trying to get on top of things

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: While Brian sets up his camera for the photoshoot in the garage, Kevin shouts that he's ready and drops his robe just as Abi enters the garage. Debbie enters the corner shop to be faced by a naked Dev, who darts between the aisles trying to cover his modesty. Billy meets up with the bishop and to Todd's horror, tells him he realises he isn't up to the job and would like to resign as archdeacon. Steve investigates Curtis's sneaky behaviour

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by fellow chef Paul Ainsworth to explore the culinary delights of Dorset, with the pair going aboard a millionaire's yacht, where James cooks a sea bass dish. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on pork and chicken cooked al fresco

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve confronts Curtis in the bistro. Curtis admits he's got it wrong and, trusting Steve with his secret, he makes him promise not to tell Emma. Summer rounds on Paul and tells him how the theft of the heat pump has cost Billy his job. Leanne instructs Sam to put his posters back on the wall, as Oliver's old bedroom is now his and she wants him to feel at home

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of two women trying to find birth parents after half a century apart. Ann Melbourne found her birth mother over a decade ago and now wants to locate her birth father Leslie, who she has limited information about in her adoption paperwork, while Amanda Village is searching for her mother Susan, who gave her up for adoption

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Robson and Jim's Icelandic Fly-Fishing Adventure
programme.desc: Actors and best friends Robson Green and Jim Murray travel across Iceland in the fishing adventure of a lifetime. The start of the trip sees them arrive in Iceland and commence their search for the much-coveted wild Atlantic salmon. However, as they arrive at the beginning of the salmon season, they discover that finding them is far more difficult than they imagined. Robson also opens up about how fly fishing and fishing in general helped him through a tough time in his life

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali, Dovey and Spooner travel to a picturesque lake set in forest land known as Iles3, or Three Islands, near Montereau south of Paris. This body of water is known for its large common carp and all three anglers hope to improve upon their best catches

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster. Some of the star names who compete in AEW include Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody, Jon Moxley, Kenny Omega, the Young Bucks, Miro, Britt Baker, and English former professional boxer Anthony Ogogo. Presented by Jim Ross, Tony Schiavone and Excalibur

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which two couples and a mother and daughter use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts help people in Blackpool sort the treasures from their trash, where items include a piece of possessed wood, a knight on horseback, and some Lalique. Henry Nicholls goes on a Tolkien-inspired epic quest, but ends up getting lost

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which teams of five compete to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jimmy and Nicola try to reconcile. Liam and Jacob argue, and David comments on a weird dynamic

programme.title: Wild China with Ray Mears
programme.desc: Ray explores plants and wildlife In China's tropical rainforest. He also meets elephant conservationist Dr Bao Mingwei, and goes in search of China's Asian elephant

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the garden makeover team head to Chippenham, Wiltshire to create a traditional formal garden for an inspirational couple. Richard Warren has been a serving Special Constable for 52 years, and dedicates his spare time to running marathons to raise money for charity, and caring for his wife Lynette, who has multiple sclerosis. The team shares tips on how to create a formal pond from scratch, screening solutions for unsightly spots in the garden, and reveals how to mark out a perfectly oval lawn

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones, Catherine Tyldesley, Denise Van Outen and AJ Odudu take to the kitchen in a series of head to head cooking battles. They each attempt to create the perfect Italian dish, and avoid elimination in the Cook Off. Presented by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Goodwood Future Lab
programme.desc: The celebration of revolutionary technology focusing on the future of living, mobility and motorsport. The documentary explores Technology for a Better World - the innovations that look to improve lives, as well as the story of the people behind them.

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry brings his Chopper rebuild to a glorious climax while taking time to ride the latest CCM bike, tell the story of the Honda Fireblade and ride out on vintage Norton ES2

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four more contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Lyndsey, Trudy, Peter and Diane must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Meena meddles. Laurel helps Nicola for her date night, and Tracy is trying to get on top of things

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: While Brian sets up his camera for the photoshoot in the garage, Kevin shouts that he's ready and drops his robe just as Abi enters the garage. Debbie enters the corner shop to be faced by a naked Dev, who darts between the aisles trying to cover his modesty. Billy meets up with the bishop and to Todd's horror, tells him he realises he isn't up to the job and would like to resign as archdeacon. Steve investigates Curtis's sneaky behaviour

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by fellow chef Paul Ainsworth to explore the culinary delights of Dorset, with the pair going aboard a millionaire's yacht, where James cooks a sea bass dish. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on pork and chicken cooked al fresco

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve confronts Curtis in the bistro. Curtis admits he's got it wrong and, trusting Steve with his secret, he makes him promise not to tell Emma. Summer rounds on Paul and tells him how the theft of the heat pump has cost Billy his job. Leanne instructs Sam to put his posters back on the wall, as Oliver's old bedroom is now his and she wants him to feel at home

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of two women trying to find birth parents after half a century apart. Ann Melbourne found her birth mother over a decade ago and now wants to locate her birth father Leslie, who she has limited information about in her adoption paperwork, while Amanda Village is searching for her mother Susan, who gave her up for adoption

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Robson and Jim's Icelandic Fly-Fishing Adventure
programme.desc: Actors and best friends Robson Green and Jim Murray travel across Iceland in the fishing adventure of a lifetime. The start of the trip sees them arrive in Iceland and commence their search for the much-coveted wild Atlantic salmon. However, as they arrive at the beginning of the salmon season, they discover that finding them is far more difficult than they imagined. Robson also opens up about how fly fishing and fishing in general helped him through a tough time in his life

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali, Dovey and Spooner travel to a picturesque lake set in forest land known as Iles3, or Three Islands, near Montereau south of Paris. This body of water is known for its large common carp and all three anglers hope to improve upon their best catches

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster. Some of the star names who compete in AEW include Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody, Jon Moxley, Kenny Omega, the Young Bucks, Miro, Britt Baker, and English former professional boxer Anthony Ogogo. Presented by Jim Ross, Tony Schiavone and Excalibur

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the garden makeover team head to Chippenham, Wiltshire to create a traditional formal garden for an inspirational couple. Richard Warren has been a serving Special Constable for 52 years, and dedicates his spare time to running marathons to raise money for charity, and caring for his wife Lynette, who has multiple sclerosis. The team shares tips on how to create a formal pond from scratch, screening solutions for unsightly spots in the garden, and reveals how to mark out a perfectly oval lawn

programme.title: Dress to Impress

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Recruitment manager Neale from Northampton, fire training officer Will from London, banker Johnny from Glasgow, and holiday-camp entertainer Ben from Essex all enter Paddy McGuinness's 'love lift'. programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking three contestants to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Comedy milestones of 1984, when cult hits like The Young Ones and Alas Smith and Jones battled it out in the schedules with more mainstream fare like Russ Abbot's Madhouse, and politicians were targeted by one of the most irreverent TV shows of all time - Spitting Image. Fans also lost two of the greatest comedy stars of all time - Tommy Cooper and Eric Morecambe. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: An Audience with Jimmy Tarbuck
programme.desc: The Liverpudlian comic performs before a host of showbiz celebrities including Cilla Black, Bruce Forsyth, Billy Connolly, Michael Parkinson, Harry Secombe and Henry Cooper. Jimmy will be fielding questions from the audience, and the event concludes with a special jam session featuring Hank Marvin, Rick Wakeman, Kenny Jones, Justin Hayward and John Lodge

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A serial killer begins targeting members of a religious group that claims to provide guidance for lost souls. As the investigation proceeds, it becomes clear that Hathaway, who once trained for the priesthood, knows more about the case than he is willing to reveal - causing Lewis to consider him as a suspect. Guest starring Ian McNeice and Rachael Stirling alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.desc: Scott arrives at the hospital to see how Kelly is. Mandy apologises to Paddy about the argument. Chris proposes he pays for Kelly to visit a private psychiatric clinic

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Diane moves into the Woolpack. Frankie thinks Zoe is giving her the cold shoulder. Roy cannot get to grips with Kelly being in a psychiatric ward

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon is abducted by an old Army friend and former Japanese PoW camp inmate, whose wife's death has left him mentally unbalanced, while Greengrass is well and truly fleeced by father-and-son con-men. Bill Maynard, Derek Fowlds and John Alderton star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl takes an overdose and Blaketon's mysterious private life comes to light when his car goes missing and is involved in an accident. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is concerned about the way Zoe is acting ahead of the court case. Marc accuses Angie and Sean of not caring when they miss parents' evening

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tension is in the air at the Sugdens between Jack, Sarah and Richie. Marlon confides in Eric that he thinks Graham killed Rachel. Frankie asks Bernice for a job at the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine spends the night with Tony. Jim gets a walking frame to help practice his standing. Steve advises Liz to cut herself free from Jim. Toyah tires of Dobber

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim makes progress at physio and starts to take small steps. Alma encourages Mike to employ Deirdre. Liz is hostile towards Michael when he returns from his job interview

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Angus, a member of the Bleakridge Watch, a group of villagers who walk the streets reporting anyone who falls foul of the law, is found dead in his meat freezer. Barnaby and Winter meet Angus's devastated sister and her daughter and learn that Angus was investigating a recent spate of burglaries in the village, but had yet to identify the culprit. Angus' post-mortem reveals strange restraining injuries on his wrists and the plot thickens as Barnaby discovers just how far some people will go for power. Frances Barber guest stars as the leader of the Watch

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Harriet Walter (Law & Order: UK) joins the cast as the costume drama returns. Following Matthew's shocking death, new mother Mary is struggling to come to terms with her loss, and Robert and Violet disagree over how to handle the situation. Downstairs, Jimmy finally takes an interest in Ivy, while Carson receives an unwelcome reminder of his past. Mrs Hughes sees a chance to help grief-stricken Isobel, and Thomas has the children's nanny in his sights. Michelle Dockery, Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, Ed Speleers and Phyllis Logan also star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of eminent lawyer Anaan Kamel is found washed up by an underground river, suspicion falls on Marcus Layton, a former employee of the victim's chambers who was recently fired by her husband Raheel. Text messages suggest an affair that had progressed to blackmail, but Banks and Morton struggle to find further information about Marcus, as his records only seem to date back five years. And when his corpse is found upstream in what looks to be a contract killing, the case becomes much more complicated

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Geoff now a marked man, he and daughter Evie are put under watch at a hotel while the search for Mullen is widened. However, when the suspect follows Evie's boyfriend to the location and sets off the fire alarm, he draws Geoff and Evie outside. Meanwhile, Banks believes loan shark Hargreaves is behind the hit on Marcus, and manages to extend his stay in the cell, despite having very little evidence against him. The conclusion of the two-part case, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress investigates missing golf balls, and some lads break into a local war hero's home. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds and William Simons

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is confused when Frankie says she is going to look for her own place. Marlon tells Kathy that there are things she does not know about Graham. Kelly tells Roy about Scott

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah are given a court date for their appeal to adopt Andy. Graham receives a mystery letter. Zak is stunned when he receives a surprise visitor

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe is upset to find her purse missing. Deirdre starts work in Mike's office. Tony runs low on his supply of drugs and orders a large amount from Jason

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah tells Dobber they should not see each other anymore. Liz breaks down at the thought of Michael leaving. Des and Natalie return from Ireland

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, giving rise to fears that the so-called 'beast', a monster enshrined in local legend, has returned to wreak fresh havoc. Kersey ventures up onto the moors to investigate and does not return - so Wycliffe is forced to go it alone. Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Winter and Barnaby investigate when Lower Pampling's star cricketer Leo Henderson is found dead after a match in the grounds, pummelled to death by cricket balls. Barnaby is surprised to see DS Jones undercover as one of Leo's team-mates, and his former colleague reveals he is working on suspected cases of match-fixing in the area, but things take an even more sinister turn when captain Fitz Theara is stabbed through the heart with a cricket stump

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary begins to emerge from her grief, and takes an interest in the running of the estate. However, her ambitions meet firm resistance from her father. Lady Rose MacClare, Lady Flintshire's daughter, finds herself in a predicament at a tea dance in York, while Mrs Hughes urges Carson to heal an old wound. Bates comes up with a plan to help Molesley, but will it work? Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Hugh Bonneville, Lily James, Phyllis Logan, Jim Carter, Brendan Coyle and Kevin Doyle

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gerald Hadleigh, the secretary of the local writers' circle in the tranquil village of Midsomer Worthy, is found battered to death on the eve of a visit by a best-selling novelist. Barnaby and Troy discover Hadleigh was a mystery man, with no relatives or marriage certificate to match the wedding photo on display in his house. To make matters worse, another body is then found. Mystery, guest starring Anna Massey and Una Stubbs

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of student Josh Tate is found dumped in a ravine, the investigation into his stabbing uncovers a very different man to the quiet character described by his flatmate Spencer. It emerges that Josh was infatuated with Spencer's girlfriend and had been manufacturing Ecstasy for club owner Liam Fallon. Meanwhile, DI Morton's ex-lover Martin Hexton has arrived, claiming to be working on a local case. When Fallon comes under suspicion as Josh's murderer the detectives break into his flat and find him badly beaten - and to complicate matters, it looks as if Hexton is responsible

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Morton works hard to win back Banks's approval and discovers Hexton is the biological father of murdered student Josh. The team traces the former undercover police officer to Gerald and Elaine Foster's house, but it's too late - Hexton has kidnapped lap-dancer Melanie. When his victim collapses, however, he takes her to hospital, where ex-lover Helen corners him and has him arrested. What will he reveal about Josh and Melanie, and the attack on club owner Liam? Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid takes a little exercise and Trevor decides to join him. Seventies sitcom, starring Sid James and Anthony Jackson

programme.title: Emmerdale Omnibus
programme.desc: Omnibus. The village grieves, Gabby and Noah bond, and David has a re-think

programme.title: George and Mildred: The Movie (1980)
programme.desc: The mismatched suburban couple go on a weekend holiday in London to celebrate their wedding anniversary, only for gangsters to mistake George for a hired killer. Spin-off from the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce, Brian Murphy and Stratford Johns

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Comedy milestones of 1984, when cult hits like The Young Ones and Alas Smith and Jones battled it out in the schedules with more mainstream fare like Russ Abbot's Madhouse, and politicians were targeted by one of the most irreverent TV shows of all time - Spitting Image. Fans also lost two of the greatest comedy stars of all time - Tommy Cooper and Eric Morecambe. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: An Audience with Jimmy Tarbuck
programme.desc: The Liverpudlian comic performs before a host of showbiz celebrities including Cilla Black, Bruce Forsyth, Billy Connolly, Michael Parkinson, Harry Secombe and Henry Cooper. Jimmy will be fielding questions from the audience, and the event concludes with a special jam session featuring Hank Marvin, Rick Wakeman, Kenny Jones, Justin Hayward and John Lodge

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A serial killer begins targeting members of a religious group that claims to provide guidance for lost souls. As the investigation proceeds, it becomes clear that Hathaway, who once trained for the priesthood, knows more about the case than he is willing to reveal - causing Lewis to consider him as a suspect. Guest starring Ian McNeice and Rachael Stirling alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.desc: Extended edition of the soap

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood takes a look back at the glory days of British Wrestling, with some classic action from the archives.

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: The latest news and reviews from the world of cars. With a new SUV from Buick and the Lexus LC500 Convertible.

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents MSUK from Thruxton with racing from Round 2 of the Mini Challenge and the Ginetta GT5 Challenge

programme.title: Goodwood Festival of Speed
programme.desc: The first of two highlights shows from the Goodwood Estate, celebrating motorsport's all-rounders, with stories of racing legends, as well as all the action, history and emotion from the festival

programme.title: Goodwood Festival of Speed
programme.desc: The second of two highlights shows from the Goodwood Estate, featuring motorsport heroes in action in iconic road and race cars, as well as global debuts of exclusive supercars. Plus, the racing action from the world famous hillclimb reaches its conclusion in the timed shootout finale

programme.title: BRDC Formula 3 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the third meeting of the campaign as the drivers go for glory in rounds seven, eight and nine at Donington Park

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali, Dovey, and Spooner travel to a picturesque water set in forest land known as Iles3, or Three Islands, near Montereau south of Paris. This lake is known for its large common carp, and all three anglers hoped to improve upon their best catches

programme.title: Robson and Jim's Icelandic Fly-Fishing Adventure
programme.desc: Actors and best friends Robson Green and Jim Murray travel across Iceland in the fishing adventure of a lifetime. The start of the trip sees them arrive in Iceland and commence their search for the much-coveted wild Atlantic salmon. However, as they arrive at the beginning of the salmon season, they discover that finding them is far more difficult than they imagined. programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 21st and final stage of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 108.4km course from Chatou to the iconic finish on the Champs-Elysees in Paris. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: From Canada's waterlogged wilderness come stories of a 20ft lake monster that attacks humans. For freshwater detective Jeremy Wade, investigation turns into obsession as he tries to explain widespread accounts of the aggressive serpent-like beast

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing 12th-century monk investigates a murder as the Abbey becomes involved in the war between King Stephen and the Empress Maud. The trail of clues leads him to a cache of hidden treasure and the fleeing daughter of the castle's owner, Godith. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Bohemia, as Holmes is outwitted by a bewitching woman's blackmail who owns a compromising photograph of a European royal. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the brilliant Baker Street sleuth, with David Burke as his trusty companion Dr Watson, guest starring Gayle Hunnicutt

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Mrs Peel infiltrate an international conference at which the delegates have begun acting strangely - by predicting their own deaths. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star, with Clifford Evans, William Lucas and Allan Cuthbertson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter stake out a dodgy garage, believed to be the base of a criminal gang. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star in the first episode of the crime-busting drama, with Ian Hendry, Brian Blessed and June Brown

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur dabbles in the world of nouvelle cuisine, taking bookings on banquets. However, his chef goes on the run from a group of gangsters leaving him in the lurch when catering for a policeman's retirement party. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Vintage restorers Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove challenge themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's unwanted automotive junk and classic collectibles. They begin at Bristol Classic Car Show, where they acquire a quirky car from the 1970s, but making a quick profit does not prove easy

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Over the next six nights, Jeremy Clarkson welcomes more contestants to the studio, all keen to answer 15 questions in the hope of winning £1,000,000 - with lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Will one lucky contestant start 2019 as a millionaire?

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley argue the case for the best affordable GT car released in 1969. Alex makes a case for the hugely popular Datsun 240Z, while Vicki drives 70's TV detectives favourite the Ford Capri. The winning car is selected by a judging panel made up of motoring experts featuring former racing driver and broadcaster Karun Chandhok, Hollywood stunt driver and author Ben Collins and top motoring presenter Rory Reid.

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley each make a case for a supercar released in 1975 that would go on to become an 80s icon. Vicki drives and champions the Ferrari 308 made famous by the Magnum PI TV series, while Alex gets behind the wheel of one of his childhood heroes, James Bond car the Lotus Esprit. Opinion and the selection of a winning car comes from a panel of former Top Gear Stig Ben Collins, broadcaster and journalist Jason Barlow and author Richard Porter

programme.title: The Bourne Ultimatum (2007)
programme.desc: Amnesiac rogue assassin Jason Bourne's long-buried memories are reawakened by a British journalist researching the secret organisation that was responsible for transforming him into a killing machine. Meanwhile, a ruthless CIA director becomes convinced Bourne is still a security risk, and plans to dispose of him once and for all. Third instalment of the spy thriller series, directed by Paul Greengrass and starring Matt Damon, Joan Allen, Julia Stiles, David Strathairn, Paddy Considine, Scott Glenn and Albert Finney

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Strange, seemingly malevolent encoded messages sour a wealthy man's wedded bliss, prompting him to seek Holmes's expertise. Victorian mystery, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The death of a ventriloquist leads to the discovery that he was carrying a collection of gems in his stomach. Meanwhile, Steed resorts to blackmail and Emma embarks on a mission in the business community

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Members of Regan's own flying squad fall under suspicion when £35,000 in cash goes missing during an operation to protect a security van. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Boxer Lewis Nelson hires Ray to keep his gambling-addicted wife away from the casino he partly owns. However, she persuades Arthur to play the roulette wheels on her behalf, tempting him with the 'winning system' she has devised. Comedy starring George Cole

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Action from October 1976, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Crystal Palace, Leeds United v Manchester United and Southampton v Fulham

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: Robson and Jim's Icelandic Fly-Fishing Adventure
programme.desc: As the best friends continue their journey driving from pool to pool across the stunning island, Jim Murray explains how fishing was his light in the darkest of times. As the journey continues and time ticks by, it remains to be seen whether Robson Green and his ally will finally track down the salmon they desperately want

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali, Dovey, and Spooner travel to a picturesque water set in forest land known as Iles3, or Three Islands, near Montereau south of Paris. This lake is known for its large common carp, and all three anglers hoped to improve upon their best catches

programme.title: Robin Hood (2010)
programme.desc: An archer fighting in Richard the Lionheart's crusading army returns home to England and assumes the identity of a nobleman killed in battle. His new-found status draws him into a dangerous conflict with members of the newly crowned King John's court, and a traitorous knight plots to aid the French in conquering England. Ridley Scott's medieval adventure, starring Russell Crowe, Cate Blanchett and Mark Strong
