programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Milhouse's parents get divorced, filling Homer with misgivings about the state of his own marriage. Determined not to drift apart from Marge, he begins making elaborate efforts to be considerate - but his efforts only make matters worse

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart runs away from home at Thanksgiving and ends up in a shelter for down-and-outs, where he is interviewed by a TV news crew - much to the horror of Homer and Marge

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Ben Miller discusses the TV series Professor T. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen talks about a campaign to design the world's best dog house. Emma Willis talks about the TV show Cooking with the Stars. Gary Kemp chats about his album, INSOLO. Actor Tchéky Karyo discusses the TV series Baptiste. Plus, music from Joy Crookes, who performs Feet Don't Fail Me Now

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An evening's car-crushing entertainment brings out the daredevil in Bart - who decides to try some death-defying stunts of his own, culminating in a skateboard jump over a gorge. Luckily, Homer is on hand to bring him down to earth

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge begins a crusade to clean up TV, convinced The Itchy & Scratchy Show is turning baby Maggie into a thug. The show's creators agree to make it less violent in future, but the move has a disastrous effect on its ratings

programme.title: Live Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents a look ahead to this afternoon's race from Silverstone. With analysis from David Coulthard, Lee McKenzie, Billy Monger, Mark Webber and Eddie Jordan

programme.title: Live Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents coverage of the 10th round of the season, which takes place at Silverstone (Start-time 3.00pm). Lewis Hamilton of Mercedes won this race for the seventh time in his career last year, but it is Red Bull's Max Verstappen who has been the dominant driver so far this season. With analysis from David Coulthard, Lee McKenzie, Billy Monger, Mark Webber and Eddie Jordan

programme.title: Live Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents a look back at the 10th round of the season, which took place at Silverstone, featuring action replays and interviews with the key figures. With analysis from David Coulthard, Lee McKenzie, Billy Monger, Mark Webber and Eddie Jordan

programme.title: Guy Martin's Best Bits
programme.desc: The presenter looks back at times when he helped to build and pilot amazing machines to world speed records, and tells the fascinating stories behind the record attempts

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The start of the wedding season sees Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree at their busiest, with events booked all through the summer, but there is still time to make improvements. Angel has her sights set on transforming the gravelled area at the front of the chateau into a thing of beauty and the whole family take a trip to Monet's stunning house and garden, where a plan for the chateau's very own water lily patch is hatched

programme.title: A Very British Country House
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following the staff of Cliveden House, a country house hotel in a Grade I listed building, during their busiest summer season. Two social media influencers are invited to stay, while wedding coordinator Lydia prepares the venue for her clients' big day. Wedding co-ordinator Lydia organises the biggest event of her career, with the couple having booked Cliveden exclusively for two days and the bride requesting staff wear traditional Indian dress

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: June seeks out more active rebels in the Chicago war zone, while Janine tries to help her fit in with their new group of survivors. Moira goes on her first field aid mission

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces, some of Britain's best loved personalities, turn their hand to being the country's most opinionated viewers, sharing their opinions on what they have been watching during the week. Taking part this time are Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Dame Maureen Lipman, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Clare Balding and Alice Arnold, Melanie C and her brother Paul, Joel Dommett and Hannah Cooper, KSI and S-X, Miquita and Andi Oliver, Kadeena Cox and Adam Gemili, Snoochie Shy and Jeremy Vine, and Nigel Havers and Denis Lawson

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents action from the 10th round of the season at Silverstone. Analysis from David Coulthard, Mark Webber, Billy Monger, Eddie Jordan and Lee McKenzie

programme.title: Fargo
programme.desc: Josto finds himself in the crosshairs of an unexpected foe, Oraetta is finally forced to come clean, Ethelrida completes her history report, and Thurman and Dibrell attempt to get back to business as usual. In the meantime, Satchel's journey comes to an end, and Loy learns an unsavoury lesson about business from Ebal

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners in Lancashire take part in the dinner party competition, hoping to secure the £1,000 prize. First to host are Rosie and her husband David, who have been married for 25 years and are seriously loved-up. Next, young newlyweds Melisa and Kyle cook together for the first time to mixed reviews. The third and final evening is led by bickering couple Gary and Linda, but the duo may need to work hard if they want to be in with a chance of winning the cash prize

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Sam Dushey, the CEO of Shoppers World, goes undercover in his own company. Since taking over the family business, Sam has made it his mission to expand Shoppers World rapidly, and he wants to make sure all of his stores are performing up to his high standards as the business grows. Among the employees he meets are a woman who has worked for the company for eight years without a pay rise, and another who was attacked with pepper spray while trying to stop a robbery

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke meets a young couple who have come up with a radical way of getting onto the housing ladder - they are building a home almost entirely from scrap on a budget of zero pounds. He also follows the progress of a woman putting together a garden retreat from reclaimed copper. The only problem is, she has no experience and suffers from rheumatoid arthritis. In Germany, George takes a futuristic houseboat out for a spin, and as the floating beach hut nears completion, there is drama in the workshop

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of house-hunters searching the Reading area. Kirstie is with Chloe, who wants a place in the trendy suburb of Caversham, and is joined on her search by her father Clive. However, after two years of looking and 60 properties viewed, the pair are struggling. Phil teams up with teachers Richard and Jo, who need to find a family home before term starts. But it needs to be a property good enough to get Richard excited

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists are up early for a morning of rock climbing on Gran Canaria - only for Brendan to announce a massive cliffhanger of his own

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Anne Robinson hosts the game show in which contestants pit their wits against each other in a battle of words and numbers. Rachel Riley looks after the board, while columnist Grace Dent joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: The couple go to the opera, but Mike fails to enjoy himself due to a cold. With her sweetheart bed-ridden, Molly decides to play nursemaid, but ends up coming into conflict with her patient's mother, who does not appear to appreciate having her role as carer usurped

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Vince tries to strike up a friendship with Mike by inviting him to a crucial basketball game, but Molly is not impressed by their new-found bond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to ensure Frank's 65th birthday is memorable by buying him an expensive aquarium just like the one he has always admired in a local Chinese restaurant. Unfortunately, one of the fish dies, sinking the Barone household into gloom and discord

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly attends a high-school reunion with Debra, only to discover she had been friends with the in-crowd who made his nerdy teenage years a living hell. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to intercept a strongly worded letter sent by Debra, telling Marie to stop interfering in her affairs. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink lands a job as guest host on a morning TV show, and finds himself considering a permanent move from radio. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles finds out that Maris has put on weight while pining for him, and Frasier throws a bachelor party for Donny - but ends up handcuffed to a stripper. Starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny and Leonard announce that they are moving in together, prompting Sheldon to revisit 2003, a much simpler time before he had met either of them. Howard and Raj form a band to play at the comic-book store, only to fall out over musical differences

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The boys want to become more sporty, so Barry Kripke leads them in a fencing lesson, while Penny, Amy and Bernadette help Stuart, who wants to attract more female customers to the comic-book store

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: In the middle of a nationwide shortage of liquid helium, Sheldon and Leonard take desperate measures to obtain the supplies they need in order to prove a scientific theory before a rival team of researchers can beat them to it. Meanwhile, Penny and Bernadette download a dating app onto Amy's phone in order to find her a new boyfriend

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and his boss Mr Burns are trapped in a mountain cabin during a survival trip with the power plant employees, and find themselves forced to work together as equals to survive - but the strain of isolation soon takes its toll

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge is diagnosed with stress, so the decision is made to hire a nanny to help her cope with the domestic demands. Successful applicant Shary Bobbins bears a striking resemblance to Mary Poppins, and soon has Bart and Lisa doing chores - but the family starts to become too dependent on her

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Scarlette Douglas offers advice to scuba-diving couple Caitlin and Simon, who want to buy a dream holiday home on the idyllic Greek island of Paros, famous for its gorgeous beaches and traditional villages. Scarlette hopes to make a splash with the five options she lines up for their £140,000 budget

Inga brings in a 1920s 'beer engine' - aka pint puller - which her husband bought on a whim a few years ago. She's now looking to get it fixed up and sold on. The final item in need of restoration is an unusual rotating bookcase, owned by student Nick

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete for the cash prize of £1,000 in Bournemouth, beginning with Nikki and Steve, followed by accountant Peter and retired air stewardess Lyn, then Nicki and Emily

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Lisa investigate Krusty's new candy, while Marge helps Homer dress for a promotion and Kent Brockman struggles with the changing media world

programme.desc: Documentary following a three-year police investigation into a human trafficking gang kidnapping women in South America to be forced to work as prostitutes in the UK. programme.desc: The first of two editions taking viewers behind the scenes of the police investigation into the premeditated and brutal murder of a young man after officers were called to woods in Hinchingbrooke Country Park in Cambridgeshire when a dog walker found a blood-soaked body. It quickly becomes clear that with no witnesses and no CCTV, there are few clues for DCI Adam Gallop to work with, and cameras follow every twist and turn of an enquiry that involves unravelling what lies behind the murder

programme.desc: The South Yorkshire Police deal with complex cases of parents living in fear of their own children. In Sheffield, a mother asks for her 15-year-old to be taken into custody after he starts causing damage at their home, and for the second time in three days, the same officers are despatched to a report of a son attacking his father. Elsewhere, a distraught mother is under siege from her violent and aggressive 23-year-old son

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The patisserie chefs face the quarter-final, and for their first challenge they serve up 24 Black Forest gateaux and 24 mini roulades. programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Itchy & Scratchy Show goes into a ratings nosedive, so the producers try to boost its appeal by adding a new canine character named Poochie, voiced by Homer. Unfortunately, the plan does not go quite as expected

Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden judge their efforts and decide which pair will be leaving one week before the final

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Anne Marie has been rushed to St George's following a suspected stroke, and staff have to act quickly to help prevent any long-lasting damage, while five-day-old Gracie is brought in by new parents Danny and Jordan, concerned she has an infected umbilical cord

Blake Edwards' comedy, starring Peter Sellers, David Niven, Capucine, Robert Wagner and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: Hail, Caesar! (2016)
programme.desc: An executive in 1950s Hollywood is employed to bury scandals, solve problems and ensure everything goes to plan for the studio. In one fraught day, he has to contend with the kidnapping of the leading man from the set of an ancient Roman epic, a musical star's secret pregnancy, and a director who objects to having a miscast actor forced on him. The Coen brothers' comedy, starring Josh Brolin, George Clooney, Scarlett Johansson and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Risen (2016)
programme.desc: A Roman officer and his assistant are charged with investigating what became of Jesus' body following the crucifixion, as the Roman hierarchy fears stories of a resurrected Messiah may lead to an uprising in Jerusalem. However, what they discover during their search forces them to question everything they believe. Biblical drama, starring Joseph Fiennes and Tom Felton

programme.title: Passengers (2016)
programme.desc: A spacecraft transporting thousands of people to a distant planet malfunctions. As woken travelling companions Jim Preston and Aurora Lane fall in love, they are soon on a collision course with more technical problems and a dark secret. Sci-fi drama, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Chris Pratt and Michael Sheen

programme.title: The Exorcism of Emily Rose (2005)
programme.desc: A student dies after an attempted exorcism by a priest who was convinced she was possessed by evil spirits. A sceptical lawyer is assigned to defend the cleric against the charge of manslaughter, but as the case unfolds she finds her own convictions challenged. Horror drama, starring Tom Wilkinson, Laura Linney, Jennifer Carpenter, Campbell Scott and Colm Feore

programme.title: Labor Day (2013)
programme.desc: A 13-year-old boy is forced to grow up fast as he helps his reclusive single mother cope with depression. They encounter a mysterious stranger who is in distress and offer to take him home - only to discover he is an escaped convict and the subject of a police manhunt. Drama based on Joyce Maynard's novel, starring Kate Winslet and Josh Brolin

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: Punk Chef Scott Garthwaite travels to Yorkshire to make sure no food goes to waste. He visits a Michelin Starred restaurant, allotment owned by a Deaf foodie and a creamery

programme.title: Appointment with Danger (1950)
programme.desc: A nun is targeted by two murderers when they realise she is the only witness to one of their killings, and it is up to postal inspector to protect her and bring the men to justice. Noir thriller, starring Phyllis Calvert, Alan Ladd and Paul Stewart

programme.title: Flying Tigers (1942)
programme.desc: An American pilot leads a squadron of volunteer aviators to help China's fledgling air force fight the Japanese during the Second World War. However, he has to contend with the consequences when the actions of an arrogant hotshot pilot lead to the death of a fellow veteran airman. Drama, starring John Wayne, John Carroll, Anna Lee and Paul Kelly

programme.title: The Far Country (1954)
programme.desc: A cowpoke is steering his cattle north to the Canadian border when a corrupt sheriff commandeers the herd. A local saloon-bar owner falls for the hapless cowboy, and with her help he recaptures his cattle. Hooking up with a wagon train, the couple head for the mining town of Dawson, but the thwarted and embittered sheriff is on their tail. Western, with James Stewart, Walter Brennan and Ruth Roman

programme.title: The Black Shield of Falworth (1954)
programme.desc: The son of a disgraced medieval nobleman is raised in poverty. With the support of a kindly earl, he gains the chance to train as a knight and reclaim his birthright, proving his worth by fighting to foil a plot to overthrow the king. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Tony Curtis, Janet Leigh, David Farrar and Barbara Rush

programme.title: The Maze Runner (2014)
programme.desc: A teen arrives in an isolated community of youngsters, with no memory of who he is or the outside world. When another new arrival brings an ominous message, he realises time is running out for the group and they must find an escape route through a deadly labyrinth if they are to survive. Sci-fi adventure based on James Dashner's novel, starring Dylan O'Brien, Kaya Scodelario and Patricia Clarkson

programme.title: The Equalizer 2 (2018)
programme.desc: Robert McCall divides his time between cab driving and looking after a young man who is mixing with a bad crowd. When a CIA friend is murdered, the former black ops agent sets out to avenge her death, and winds up fighting for survival in his hurricane-battered home town. Action thriller, starring Denzel Washington, Pedro Pascal and Ashton Sanders

A British soldier deployed to Belfast in 1971, almost immediately finds himself caught up in a riot. In the aftermath, he becomes separated from the rest of his unit. Lost and alone at night in a hostile and unfamiliar area, he makes a disturbing journey in search of safety. Thriller, starring Jack O'Connell and Richard Dormer

programme.title: The Ploughman's Lunch (1983)
programme.desc: A group of people working in the media find their cynicism grows following the Falklands War. Richard Eyre's insightful and thought-provoking drama, written by Ian McEwan and starring Jonathan Pryce, Tim Curry and Rosemary Harris

programme.title: The Black Tent (1956)
programme.desc: A wounded British Army captain is saved by a tribe of nomads in the Libyan desert during the Second World War, and they take him to their camp, where he is cared for by the sheikh's daughter and falls in love with her. Romantic drama, with Anthony Steel, Anna-Maria Sandri, Donald Sinden, Andre Morell and Donald Pleasence

programme.title: Esther Waters (1948)
programme.desc: A kitchen maid succumbs to the advances of a caddish stable lad and falls pregnant - but is abandoned for another woman. Period drama, starring Dirk Bogarde and Kathleen Ryan

programme.title: The Rare Breed (1966)
programme.desc: An English widow decides to fulfil her cattle farmer husband's final wish of introducing the Hereford breed to America. She finds a cattleman in Texas willing to help, but first she and her daughter must transport their prize bull across the wilderness, with the aid of a sceptical ranch hand. Romantic comedy Western, starring Maureen O'Hara and James Stewart

programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles (1959)
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson are asked to protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskerville family, who is believed to be the target of a monstrous spectral hound roaming the remote Devon moors. Hammer version of Arthur Conan Doyle's mystery, starring Peter Cushing, Andre Morrell, Christopher Lee, John Le Mesurier and Miles Malleson

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance

programme.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015)
programme.desc: The children who escaped from an isolated community at the centre of a mysterious labyrinth find themselves in a desolate wasteland. As they seek the truth about the organisation that imprisoned them, they face a series of terrible dangers. Sci-fi adventure based on the second of James Dashner's series of novels, starring Dylan O'Brien and Kaya Scodelario

programme.title: Snowpiercer (2013)
programme.desc: In a snow-ravaged world, a group of travellers fight for survival on a train that perpetually circumnavigates the globe. The vehicle's occupants are divided into a military ruling elite and their dishevelled captives. But now, 17 years after the train set out on its seemingly never-ending journey, the downtrodden, abused captives have had enough. Revolution is afoot. Bong Joon Ho's fantasy thriller, starring Chris Evans, Song Kang-ho, Tilda Swinton, Jamie Bell and Octavia Spencer

programme.title: The Guilty (2018)
programme.desc: Asger, a frustrated and troubled cop, is mired in a mysterious workplace controversy and has been confined to the emergency response help lines while the issue is resolved. He gets a confusing call from a woman named Iben, who appears to be in immediate danger after being abducted by her partner. Asger tries to piece together where she is and how to get help to her. Writer/director Gustav Moller's crime drama, starring Jakob Cedergren. In Danish with English subtitles

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: A family secret turns the Maaliks' world upside down, while Martine struggles to hide her cancer diagnosis, and Yazz and Tom have a plan to raise money for charity

programme.title: The SpongeBob Movie: Sponge Out of Water (2015)
programme.desc: The cheery sea creature tries to prevent a precious secret formula from falling into the wrong hands - but when it goes missing, the end of the world approaches. SpongeBob and his friends embark on a quest to get it back, which leads to them developing superpowers and venturing onto dry land. Animated comedy, with the voices of Tom Kenny and Antonio Banderas

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This contest takes place in and around Swindon and Marlborough, where the first to host is super-confident software salesman Simon, who hopes to impress with a fun night full of charm and hearty, home-cooked meals. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second to host in the West Country is business consultant Linda, who is looking to impress her guests with a game menu of pheasant, venison and pastry swans

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Parisienne Saleha hosts the third dinner party of the week in Swindon and Marlborough, serving up a largely vegetarian meal, but with a side of steak tartare

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in the West Country, retired military police officer Debs plans to treat her guests to a medieval feast of local legends including 'flying monk', 'fish platter' and 'lamb leg of barmaid-gobbled tiger'

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of Finn McCool's, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, New York. The chances of the establishment becoming a success are being ruined by the owner Buddy Mazzio's two sons, bartender Jason and chef Brian, who are constantly fighting with each other, prompting Gordon to have a heart-to-heart with the family

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of the Olde Stone Mill restaurant in Tuckahoe, New York, which is struggling to attract customers, leading to debts of nearly $1million. Gordon feels the owner is more interested in his own appearance than how his food tastes and a screaming match ensues

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny share an intimate moment as they recover from a psychological deconstruction by the physicist's mother, an expert in psychiatry and neural sciences. Guest starring Mamma Mia! actress Christine Baranski

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny starts her own hair accessories business and the orders start to pile in. However, she makes the mistake of asking Sheldon to help, leading to frustration and regret. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Deep Impact (1998)
programme.desc: A TV reporter investigating a presidential misdemeanour stumbles on evidence that a comet is on a collision course with Earth, threatening the future of the human race. Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted challenges, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their baking skills

programme.title: Daddy's Home (2015)
programme.desc: A mild-mannered man hopes for his wife's two children to view him as their father. His dreams of perfect family life are threatened by the return of their biological father, a charismatic, motorcycle-riding tough guy, and he pulls out all the stops to prove to his stepchildren that he can be just as cool. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell and Mark Wahlberg

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Ryder and Mel come to an agreement, and Joe offers his help after Mel's attempt to teach Lennox how to drive does not go down well. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox tries to get out of gym class, leading her teacher to make wild assumptions, and Mel is anxious when her mother comes to visit. Meanwhile, Joe tries to smooth over the troubled relationship between the two women. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Baby Daddy

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley looks forward to meeting Philip's sister when she visits from England, but her excitement vanishes when it becomes clear his sibling is unimpressed with his taste in girlfriends. Meanwhile, Danny's contract negotiations with the Rangers are put in jeopardy. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry and Erica forget to buy Beverly a Mother's Day gift and make last-minute 'Mom Coupons' instead. Adam yearns to go to a summer camp for junior astronauts and accuses his father of crushing his dreams when he isn't keen to give permission

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry's favourite shirt is hated by both his mother Beverly and his girlfriend Lainey, so they hatch a plan together to destroy it. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Black-ish

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Sheldon becomes jealous when Amy gets attention in the media, Raj spots Anu talking to her ex-boyfriend, and Stuart is intimidated by the idea of moving in with Denise, all of which cause mayhem when Penny and Leonard organise a paintball game

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny's former boyfriend Zack and his wife encounter fertility problems when wanting a baby, so ask Leonard for his help. Raj attempts to patch things up with his estranged fiancee Anu

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon wins a grant to pay for a summer expedition to the North Pole, and invites his friends to join him. However, the gang's imminent departure causes Leonard and Penny to reconsider their feelings for each other. Johnny Gale and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon returns to Texas in disgrace when he discovers his friends tampered with the Arctic expedition data after growing tired of his quest for a scientific breakthrough. Elsewhere, Leonard becomes frustrated as constant interruptions hamper his attempts to woo Penny

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly talks Erica into writing a Hanukkah song for the school holiday concert, inspiring Barry to prove his own musical prowess. Adam discovers that the things he loved as a child aren't always as good as he remembers. Ana Gasteyer (Suburgatory) guest stars

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly hopes to inspire the students when she takes over as substitute teacher in Barry's chemistry class. Adam accidentally embarrasses Emmy when he is forced to read out a note about her in class, so he enlists Erica's help to become a better friend. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre knows when to choose his battles with Bow, but they disagree on where to send Devante to pre-school. Junior helps Jack and Diane pull off an epic eighth-grade prank

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre realises he needs to intervene when Pops starts gambling and partying to mask his heartbreak. Bow hands school drop-off onto Junior so that she can avoid an annoying parent

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake thinks Boyle might have met his soulmate in Genevieve, an art gallery owner, and tries to set the pair up on a date. Meanwhile, Rosa deals with an intra-precinct theft and Amy seeks Holt's help with an image campaign. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: While investigating a serial killer, Peralta enlists Holt's help in an attempt to solve the case without the new captain finding out. Diaz and Santiago are forced to deal with the murderer's demands, while Jeffords discovers a new obsession. Comedy, guest starring Kyra Sedgwick and Dean Winters, with Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Barry Kripke finds proof that Dr Pemberton plagiarised his thesis in college, Sheldon and Amy aren't sure whether they should turn their rival in or not. Howard is surprised but happy to learn that Bernadette wasn't the only waitress at The Cheesecake Factory who had a crush on him back in the day

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is pleasantly surprised when his mother Beverly arrives and is genuinely kind to him - until he finds out the real reason for her visit. Denise and Stuart overstay their welcome at Howard and Bernadette's. Christine Baranski guest stars

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lady of leisure Jill is second to host in and around Blackpool and Preston, leaving no stone unturned in her effort to impress her guests with an Italian-themed menu

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean
programme.desc: Motor yacht Sirocco braces for its most exclusive charter guest yet, a new third stewardess joins the boat who has Colin swooning, and Hannah hopes she can fill Anastasia's shoes

programme.title: Ted 2 (2015)
programme.desc: The foul-mouthed talking teddy bear is horrified to discover that he counts as property according to the law. He and his human best friend hire a pot-smoking lawyer to help them fight a court case and get Ted legally recognised as a person. Comedy sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg and Amanda Seyfried, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Twenty six-year-old psychology student Alesia is tired of meeting 'boys' and is now seeking someone more emotionally mature than her previous partners. Next up is Louise from London, who has travelled the world and been on over 200 dates, but is still no closer to finding her soul mate. Anna Richardson assists both singletons, inviting them to choose their favourite partner from a line-up of potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time until fully nude. The contenders then don their glad rags to enjoy a date together

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Opinionated TV viewers share their critiques to TV from April 2017, including Kitchen 999: Emergency Chefs, the final of The Voice, Alone with the In-Laws, Line of Duty, Life Story, One Born Every Minute and the News

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Songwriter Kristina meets wedding photographer Nicola, but flirtation turns to faux pas as the drinks flow, while cheeky charmer Billy tries to win over high-society make-up artist Olivia. Self-proclaimed geek James has a date with Holly, who is physically shaking with nerves when she arrives and is looking for someone to boost her confidence

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: Rick battles with the sinister owner of the second-hand shop where Summer has a job, but Summer is upset about his interference. Jerry helps Morty with his science project about the solar system, but they can't agree on Pluto's planetary status. Guest starring Alfred Molina, Nolan North and Rich Fulcher

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: When 27 Ricks from alternate dimensions are murdered and their respective Mortys are kidnapped, Rick is framed for committing the crimes and must go after culprit - resulting in a fall-out with Morty

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Opinionated TV viewers share their critiques to TV from April 2017, including Kitchen 999: Emergency Chefs, the final of The Voice, Alone with the In-Laws, Line of Duty, Life Story, One Born Every Minute and the News

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Ted believes he has met his perfect woman at a wedding, but they do not exchange surnames or numbers, making it all but impossible for him to track her down. To complicate matters, it also transpires that Robin has developed feelings for him. Comedy, starring Josh Radnor and Cobie Smulders

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Barney receives a video from the former girlfriend who broke his heart and turned him into a suit-wearing bachelor. The gang wants to know why they split up, but he forces everyone to confide their most embarrassing moments before revealing the full story

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Mojito, a Cuban cafe in Brooklyn, New York. The restaurant was the shared dream of two newlyweds who have separated since its opening, and whose constant public quarrelling is distracting staff and driving customers away

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates the popular belief that coffee can sober people up and also looks into new research linking consumption of the beverage and a reduced risk of liver disease. In San Francisco, Kate Quilton investigates trendy new drink kombucha, while Matt Tebbutt examines why supermarket labels don't say which cut of beef thin-cut steaks come from

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke and master craftsman Will Hardie embark on a road trip to explore the ingenious small-space wonders hidden away among the snowy peaks of the Swiss, French and Italian Alps. They start out from an observation point 4,000 metres above sea level, then take in a secret ski lodge disguised as a boulder, a treehouse resembling a giant acorn and a Swiss cabin made almost entirely from concrete. Other highlights include a hamlet made from eight 200-year-old chalets collected from all over the Alps and a mountaintop restaurant resembling a spaceship

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: George challenges innovative architect Greg Blee with transforming an impractical, upside down house with a mishmash of living spaces for Martijn and Sally. The building is so badly laid out that to get from the kitchen to the garden you must go through the master bedroom, but can Greg create a design that works on Martyn and Sally's budget of £65,000?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke meets single father Rick, whose four-storey terrace in Stratford-upon-Avon has just one room per floor and is totally impractical when his children come to stay. Can the architect come up with a clever redesign to create space and much-needed character? He also helps single mum Donna in Hastings, whose Victorian double reception room has not been touched for 50 years

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition gets under way at the Oak Tree Cottage in Burley, Hampshire, where owner Jacqui Castle and her sidekick Jane Waters hope the homely approach will win the day. Guests Martin and Catherine Crosswaite are not encouraged by the shared facilities, while breakfast served at a communal table is not to the liking of Norman Butcher and Lorraine Watkins

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Bath's the Rising Sun Inn, where owners Lorraine Hawkins and Norman Butcher hope to bring their guests up to standard by having a dress code. Following their feedback on the last visit, Jacqui and Jane take an extra critical eye to the rooms, while Martin and Catherine show their competitive streak during a boules match

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Busfeild Arms in East Morton for the third day of the contest, where hosts Martin and Catherine Croswaite pride themselves in giving great Yorkshire hospitality. Jacqui is overwhelmed by the luxury and extras in the rooms, which raises questions about how the owners make a profit. Meanwhile, Martin shocks the group by sharing his stunt motorbike riding past, and later, Patrick feels the pressure of being the final host

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stopover is at Patrick Duggan's Beatles-themed Epstein House in Liverpool, the former home of band manager Brian Epstein's father. Jacqui and Jane are unimpressed by unpleasant smells in the bedroom, while Martin and Catherine show they will do anything to win during a game of Fab Four charades. At dinner, Patrick shocks his guests when he reveals his idea of the ideal woman. Will it have an effect on their feedback?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Jacqui and Jane from the Oak Tree Cottage want to find out why Norman and Lorraine wouldn't return, while Martin and Catherine from the Busfeild Arms have some explaining to do when it comes to their profit margins. Accusations fly around the table as payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Couple Mariam and Johnno arrive at their new chateau with their teenage son Monty and discover that they have no running water and that builders are busy in every room. To get respite from the heat and building work, Mariam visits Angel to get help turning her designs into tiles for her new home. Abbie and her mum Karen have wallpaper decisions to make, and wrangle oversized cots as they continue to get their chateau ready for the biggest wedding party of the season

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul desperately need to fill their lake with fresh water ready for the artists' retreat they are hosting. Emma steps in to model Victorian outfits for the artists, while Paul struggles on kitchen duty. Angel gives Abbie and Karen tips on how to save money for their wedding business, and she shows them how to create a stunning bar

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Bell House Hotel in Wiltshire, where business partners Chris Williams and Jacqui Hogg hope guests will appreciate their attempts to bring London chic to the shires. Susan and David Speak are frustrated by the low shower pressure, while Jade Hendon butts heads with Matthew over healthy eating at dinner and the hosts' absence at breakfast the next morning gets the criticism flowing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Partridge in Oxfordshire, where young couple Matthew Cockman and Paula Szkwarkowska hope guests will get on board with their passion for local organic food. A communal bathroom has the guests lost for words, and the lack of a hot breakfast included in the price leaves a bad taste in everyone's mouths

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Strawberry Gardens B&B in Heysham for the third day of the contest, where husband and wife team Susan and David Speak offer home-from-home accommodation and an abundance of northern hospitality. Sports enthusiast David treats his guests to a traditional darts competition, adjudicated by former world champion Alan Warriner-Little, but he is unimpressed when Paula sends back her evening meal. However, it is Chris and Jacqui's feedback that disappoints the hosts the most

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Bateman's Mill near Chesterfield, where manager Jade Hendon has high hopes of being the best. But she faces an uphill battle when David and Susan go out of their way to find hairs in the bathroom - and to make mattters worse, her Derbyshire breakfast is not a hit with Chris and Jacqui

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores, starting with Chris and Jacqui, owners of the Bell House, who want to find out why Matthew marked them down for being 'too tall' for his bed. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Cameras follow the paramedic teams of West Midlands Ambulance Service, beginning with a crew treating a man found collapsed in his garden in Birmingham. In Coventry, a teenage boy discovers his mum unconscious on the kitchen floor and rings for an ambulance, and in Worcester, paramedics are called to a seven-month-old baby girl with a high temperature who is struggling to breathe

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Domestic burglaries in Wiltshire have risen by more than 20 per cent in the past year and this edition explores what lies behind this troubling statistic, looking at the police's struggle to support victims and try to catch the culprits. In Swindon, a burglar is caught red-handed stealing food from someone's freezer, while a 999 call is received from a terrified single woman who has been woken by the sound of breaking glass to find to her horror that her home is being ransacked while she is in her bed

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man is brought in with stomach pains and a suspected abdominal aortic aneurysm. His son receives a huge blow when he is told his father only has a 50 per cent chance of surviving the operation he urgently needs. A 33-year-old is treated after swallowing a cocktail of drugs in a suicide attempt, and staff fight to save a teenager who was stabbed in a street fight and has lost a life-threatening amount of blood

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Malcolm, who has had a cardiac arrest, is put under emergency anaesthetic on the hospital helipad. Helicopter medics rush to help elderly driver Bill after his car flips in Whitley Bay. In Oxfordshire, a sidecar race comes to an abrupt end for brothers Paul and Shane

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Cameras follow the paramedic teams of West Midlands Ambulance Service, beginning with a crew treating a man found collapsed in his garden in Birmingham. In Coventry, a teenage boy discovers his mum unconscious on the kitchen floor and rings for an ambulance, and in Worcester, paramedics are called to a seven-month-old baby girl with a high temperature who is struggling to breathe

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Domestic burglaries in Wiltshire have risen by more than 20 per cent in the past year and this edition explores what lies behind this troubling statistic, looking at the police's struggle to support victims and try to catch the culprits. In Swindon, a burglar is caught red-handed stealing food from someone's freezer, while a 999 call is received from a terrified single woman who has been woken by the sound of breaking glass to find to her horror that her home is being ransacked while she is in her bed

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man is brought in with stomach pains and a suspected abdominal aortic aneurysm. His son receives a huge blow when he is told his father only has a 50 per cent chance of surviving the operation he urgently needs. A 33-year-old is treated after swallowing a cocktail of drugs in a suicide attempt, and staff fight to save a teenager who was stabbed in a street fight and has lost a life-threatening amount of blood

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal allows himself to be led astray by rebellious new arrival Father Damien, and Ted is suspected of being the criminal mastermind behind a scandalous theft. Comedy, with Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Joe Rooney

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the single-cake class at the Devon County Show, and hopes to win over the judges with her own Caribbean-inspired recipe. However, it is a fellow competitor's beetroot cake that ends up raising their eyebrows

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Return of the programme following the work of probate detectives as they try to find people who are unknowingly heirs, sometimes to substantial fortunes. In the first edition, the researchers unearth the history of Croydon's perfume industry as they trace the relatives of a woman who died in the seaside town of Poole, Dorset. Plus, two estranged brothers, who grew up unaware of the other's existence, meet for the first time

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a Yorkshire textile mill, where Simon repurposes a mill trolley into a coffee table and Henry struggles to repair a Soviet-era motorbike

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Surrey and Hampshire. Simon builds a huge kitchen table from discarded rafters and gives a 1970s campervan a new lease of life. Henry hankers after an American tractor and Royal Enfield motorbike and sidecar

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend get help from actor James Nesbitt as they try to fix up a 1959 MG MGA sports car that has been kept in a damp barn for more than 40 years

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The temperatures soar as Dick and Angel face the busiest period of the year, as the school holidays coincide with peak wedding season. Dick comes up with an ingenious solution to stop wedding cakes from melting in the heat, but it involves piercing the chateau's walls. The moat is transformed into a boating lake, giving visitors a chance to cool down, and the family takes to the water in a home-made punt

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at David and Ewa Woodward's Keats Cottage in Shanklin on the Isle of Wight. In the rooms, Donna Taylor from Jasmine House in Leicestershire makes a hair-raising discovery, while mother-and-daughter team Lesley and Chloe Priestly take a dislike to one of their competitors at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Jasmine House in Lutterworth, Leicestershire managed by Donna Taylor who believes in a no frills approach. But it doesn't take long for Martin and Sara to realise all the added extras in the room don't add up. During the afternoon Leslie and Chloe show their fighting spirit during a kickboxing lesson and all the B&Bers square up to their host

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the luxury Halliwell House in Cheshire for the third day of the contest, where owners Martin Hulme and Sara Williams charge nearly £500 per night. Donna is astonished at the room rates, while David and Ewa are expecting nothing but perfection, but the self-service approach at breakfast confuses the guests, leaving Martin and Sara defiant in the face of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at Soar Chapel Guest House in Ebbw Vale, the family home of mum and daughter Lesley and Chloe Priestly. Expectations are high for this duo and one guest in particular is not disappointed. Things get going in the afternoon during a beatboxing contest, although old tensions resurface at dinner and the hosts are left to calm things down. A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Ash Brownrigg has skied all over the world and always dreamed of owning a log home like the ones he's seen in the snow of the Alps. He even tried building one himself in the past, but sold it many years ago. Now he's found the perfect spot at the base of Mount Ruapehu on the North Island, and is getting a new log home constructed by a company that uses traditional techniques

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help the Clayton-Fishers, who have fallen out of love with their home in Walthamstow, east London. Sarah and her wife Pepper have opposing views on what to do with their two-bedroom terrace, as Sarah thinks it is time to move on, but Pepper is digging her heels in and wants to convert the loft

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: A look back at the transformation of Dick Strawbridge and his wife Angel's 45-room chateau from an abandoned ruin into a sumptuous family home. This episode looks back at how the abandoned walled garden stole Dick and Angel's hearts and how the family members fell for the country lifestyle, with their children growing up learning about seasons, livestock and wildlife

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets a pair of civil-partnered university professors in Hertfordshire who have decided to build their first home from scratch. In addition to all the complications this usually entails, Celia Brackenridge and Diana Woodward have also enlisted the services of industrial designers who have developed a new computer-assisted method for cutting precision-engineered building blocks from scratch. However, though the technique may be clever, its creators have never created a building before - meaning everyone involved with the project is taking a giant leap into the unknown

programme.title: Full Bloom
programme.desc: The team leaders tackle a fashion forward challenge. The florists pull together eye-catching window displays fit for streetwear designer Melody Ehsani's premiere storefront

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: Daily life on the mammoth cruise ship the Symphony of the Seas, which boasts 20 pools and jacuzzis, two surf simulators, a rock-climbing wall, an ice rink, a basketball court and a mini golf course. It's all hands on deck for the crew members as 6,680 passengers arrive for the holiday of a lifetime

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man with schizophrenia is admitted for the 19th time in a year after failing to take his diabetes medication. Meanwhile, a former betting-shop manager and a 16-year-old both suffer suspected strokes - the 61-year-old, once known as the 'memory man', is now coming to terms with his diminishing mental capabilities, while the teenager's identical twin and mother wait by his bedside for the results of a brain scan

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on special guest team captain Alan Carr and Catherine Tate in the words-and-numbers quiz, while Adam Buxton joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Full Bloom
programme.desc: The team leaders tackle a fashion forward challenge. The florists pull together eye-catching window displays fit for streetwear designer Melody Ehsani's premiere storefront

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: Daily life on the mammoth cruise ship the Symphony of the Seas, which boasts 20 pools and jacuzzis, two surf simulators, a rock-climbing wall, an ice rink, a basketball court and a mini golf course. It's all hands on deck for the crew members as 6,680 passengers arrive for the holiday of a lifetime

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty sets out to learn how kippers are produced, and finds out why this nutritious smoked fish is no longer a breakfast favourite. Kate Quilton is in Germany to investigate gherkins, discovering a space-age machine that can pickle food in seconds. Matt Tebbutt and Helen Lawal try to discover why rice cakes are white when they are made of brown rice

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers try to track down the next of kin of a Newcastle man with a £100,000 estate who failed to leave a will, while another search unveils an intriguing story about London chimney sweeps in the early 20th century. Plus, details of unclaimed estates that are still in need of heirs

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The programme heads to Lowestoft in Suffolk, where Henry Cole picks a valuable Lyons Tea enamel sign while Simon O'Brien converts a station trolley into a garden sofa

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team rummage through the hangar of a Vulcan bomber, where Henry finds a cockpit seat to restore, and Simon attempts to turn part of a jet engine into a coffee table

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore travel to Worcestershire to pick up classic car fan Lee's 1965 VW Karmann Ghia, a once-stylish German motor that has now passed its sell-by date. Before they can return the restored vehicle to its surprised owner, the team must get to work replacing the starter motor and repairing the floorpan welding, accident-damaged body work and leaking engine

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The wedding season draws to close, but Dick and Angel need to look ahead to the winter. Dick ploughs up the wild flower meadow, a change that Angel does not entirely approve of, and the couple transform the dilapidated coach house into a cafe, complete with a striking neon sign

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week comes from Cliff House Hotel in Southbourne, where owner Rich Norris and chef Matt Head hope their decor and top-notch breakfast will give them the edge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second venue of the week is the Hotel Barge Waternimf in Ely, Cambridgeshire, which has an open-plan kitchen leaving the hosts exposed to everything the guests have to say

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Montpellier Hotel in Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where Hannah Henewaa and Ibrahim Sani hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Fiancees and live-in landlords Paul Wright and Dave McDermott welcome their rivals to the Coach and Horses in Compton in West Sussex, where they hope that their touch of country class will be the cream of the crop. When the guests arrive, Hannah and Ibrahim drink it all in, but the decor comes up short for Matt and Rich and a bad night's sleep doesn't leave Ibrahim in high spirits. At breakfast, the fry-up falls flat for some of the guests, and the feedback leaves a bitter taste in the hosts' mouths

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. First up are Rich and Matt from the Cliff House Hotel, and after tackling Paul and Dave's complaints about the lack of blinds in the bathroom, it's another pair's payment that could prove curtains for their chances in the competition

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Chris Moller meets Sky Mason who wants a sanctuary for himself and his two young children, so he is constructing a remote off-grid home on Slipper Island. But getting to the site is a hard enough job, braving rough weather and the dangerous Tairua sandbar

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Tuck family in Long Eaton, Derbyshire, who have grown weary of their cramped, three bedroom semi-detached home. Kirstie offers her thoughts on ways the family could change the downstairs of the property, while Phil sets out to find a suitable house they could buy instead

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree enter their fifth year of living in France. With more weddings and events booked in than ever, the couple prepare for their busiest year yet. In order to feed a total of 1,500 guests, Dick and Angel begin renovating the back of the basement, creating a brand new, state-of-the-art events kitchen. Engineer Dick faces a complicated plumbing issue and there's a kitchen design challenge for Angel

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Mary Martin and Carl Turner want to build a home that resembles a giant stack of glass cubes in Brixton, south London. The couple are hoping the structure will become an instant landmark, while on the inside they are planning a Zen retreat. But to succeed, their finances must be pushed to their limit, so Carl takes on as many of the specialist jobs as he can to save money. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Newly retired 55-year-old boating enthusiast Colin Stewart, who survived a heart attack 15 years ago, is embarking on a bold life adventure. He is planning to spend half the year cruising Britain's waterways on a high-spec narrowboat, called Tickin Along

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Paul Foot take on Jon Richardson and Angela Barnes in the words-and-numbers game, while poet John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the A&E department of London's King's College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. Also brought in by helicopter is a father-of-four who fell from scaffolding as he repaired a roof, while other cases include former docker Bill, who has hurt his knee while drinking at his local working men's club

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Newly retired 55-year-old boating enthusiast Colin Stewart, who survived a heart attack 15 years ago, is embarking on a bold life adventure. He is planning to spend half the year cruising Britain's waterways on a high-spec narrowboat, called Tickin Along

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the A&E department of London's King's College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. Also brought in by helicopter is a father-of-four who fell from scaffolding as he repaired a roof, while other cases include former docker Bill, who has hurt his knee while drinking at his local working men's club

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates what makes fizzy sweets effervesce and swots up on his chemistry to see if he can harness their explosive power in a rocket. In Ireland, Kate Quilton finds out if a range of vitamin-enriched mushrooms could help the one in five Britons who lack vitamin D, while Matt Tebbutt explores claims that raw kale may be bad for people's health

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: As Mike and Frank continue their pick through Alabama, Danielle finds them a collector with a shocking stash of high voltage oddities

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Twenty Twelve co-stars Hugh Bonneville and Jessica Hynes pair up again to go on a road trip through the former Soviet republic of Georgia. Driving from the border with Azerbaijan to the Black Sea, Jessica soon discovers her spirit of adventure is causing Hugh a few moments of high anxiety, but there is worse to come when they find themselves stuck on a high mountain pass in bad weather and he has to overcome his vertigo. While the drive is a challenge all the way, the country and its people delight and surprise them at every turn

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade takes actress and writer Jessica Hynes on a whirlwind weekend away to Iceland, a country famous for spectacular water features, geo-thermic pools and a curious belief in elves - all attributes that the pair investigate with ruthless efficiency. They go on the popular Golden Circle Tour of the Nordic island's most famous waterfalls, glaciers and geysers - by helicopter, saving seven valuable hours. They also eat rotten shark and Brenavin, a spirit made from mashed potato, and attend elf school, followed by a speedy boat trip in search of whales. There's also a rapid chill-out at the Blue Lagoon geothermic pool and spa

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedy actor Stephen Mangan for a weekend in the Moroccan city. Wanting to experience its famous street food, the pair sample a local delicacy of steamed sheep's head - an eye-opening culinary experience for them both. They then take a hot-air balloon flight over the Atlas Mountains with breathtaking views, watch a traditional story-telling performance and visit the historic Saadian tombs, as well as Marrakech's ancient tanneries. Finally, in search of a suitable place to sum up the holiday experience, Richard and Stephen head into the desert for a contemplative camel ride
